video_title: S1 01+ 巨人思維 Giant Mindset
description: 台灣知名交易員巨人傑的首部著作，記錄了他14年的股市交易心路歷程。巨人傑從17歲進入股市，以一萬元本金起步，累計成交量破五千億。本書強調「交易不是戰勝市場，而是戰勝自己」，分享如何尋找「風險報酬不對等」的機會，並建立嚴格的交易紀律。全書版稅捐贈予家扶基金會。
author: 巨人傑
description_cn: 台灣知名交易員巨人傑的首部著作，記錄了他14年的股市交易心路歷程。巨人傑從17歲進入股市，以一萬元本金起步，累計成交量破五千億。本書強調「交易不是戰勝市場，而是戰勝自己」，分享如何尋找「風險報酬不對等」的機會，並建立嚴格的交易紀律。全書版稅捐贈予家扶基金會。
description_en: The debut work of Giant Jay, a well-known Taiwanese trader, documenting his 14-year journey in the stock market. Starting at the age of 17 with a principal of just 10,000 TWD, Giant Jay accumulated a trading volume exceeding 500 billion TWD. This book emphasizes that 'trading is not about beating the market, but beating yourself,' sharing how to identify opportunities of 'asymmetric risk-reward' and establish strict trading discipline. All royalties from this book are donated to the Taiwan Fund for Children and Families. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 巨人傑 14 年股市交易的心路歷程
如何尋找風險與報酬不對等的獲利機會
交易紀律的建立與資金控管實務
戰勝交易中的人性盲點與心態修煉
quotes: 成功交易者的秘訣不在於戰勝市場，而在於徹底戰勝自我人性的貪婪與恐懼。
建立一套嚴格的風險報酬紀律，是用時間賺取複利最堅實的護城河。
quotes_en: The secret of a successful trader lies not in beating the market, but in conquering one's own greed and fear.
Establishing a strict risk-reward discipline is the most solid moat to earn compound interest over time.
outline_en: Giant Jay's 14-Year Mental Journey in Stock Trading
How to Find Profit Opportunities with Asymmetric Risk-Reward
Establishing Trading Discipline and Asset Management Practice
Overcoming Human Weaknesses and Refining Mindset in Trading

video_title: S1 02 底層邏輯 I
description: 中國商業顧問劉潤的代表作，匯集《劉潤．五分鐘商學院》中關於底層邏輯的精華。全書分為五大章節：是非對錯、思考問題、個體進化、理解他人與社會協作的底層邏輯。強調在千變萬化的世界中，唯有掌握不變的「底層邏輯」加上環境變數，才能洞察事物本質，做出正確判斷。
author: 劉潤
description_cn: 中國商業顧問劉潤的代表作，匯集《劉潤．五分鐘商學院》中關於底層邏輯的精華。全書分為五大章節：是非對錯、思考問題、個體進化、理解他人與社會協作的底層邏輯。強調在千變萬化的世界中，唯有掌握不變的「底層邏輯」加上環境變數，才能洞察事物本質，做出正確判斷。
description_en: The representative work of renowned Chinese business consultant Run Liu, compiling the essence of underlying logic from his popular column 'Run Liu: 5-Minute Business School.' The book is divided into five core chapters: the underlying logic of right and wrong, problem-solving, individual evolution, understanding others, and social collaboration. It emphasizes that in an ever-changing world, only by mastering the immutable 'underlying logic' and adapting to environmental variables can one see through to the essence of things and make correct decisions. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 解構是非對錯的多元立場與博弈
思考問題的底層邏輯與系統思考
個體進化與個人價值的重新定義
理解他人與社會協作的本質規律
quotes: 事物在變，但底層邏輯不變。只有洞察本質，才能在變動中做出正確抉擇。
是非對錯沒有絕對的標準，核心在於你站在誰的立場上進行利益博弈。
quotes_en: Things change, but the underlying logic remains. Only by grasping the essence can you make correct decisions in times of change.
There is no absolute standard for right and wrong; it depends on whose perspective you take in the game.
outline_en: Deconstructing Positions and Games of Right and Wrong
Underlying Logic and Systems Thinking in Problem Solving
Individual Evolution and Redefining Personal Value
The Core Rules of Understanding Others and Social Collaboration

title_cn: 華頓商學院最受歡迎的談判課
video_title: S1 03 華頓商學院最受歡迎的談判課 Getting More
description: 華頓商學院最受歡迎的談判課教授 Stuart Diamond 的經典著作。本書挑戰傳統談判中的「贏或輸」思維，提倡理解對方需求、建立信任關係，並透過創造價值來達成雙贏。書中涵蓋情緒智慧、準備策略、框架設定等實用技巧，適用於商業談判及日常人際互動。
author: Stuart Diamond
description_cn: 華頓商學院最受歡迎的談判課教授 Stuart Diamond 的經典著作。本書挑戰傳統談判中的「贏或輸」思維，提倡理解對方需求、建立信任關係，並透過創造價值來達成雙贏。書中涵蓋情緒智慧、準備策略、框架設定等實用技巧，適用於商業談判及日常人際互動。
description_en: Written by Professor Stuart Diamond from Wharton School of Business, this book shares numerous classroom cases explaining how to use negotiation techniques to achieve maximum benefits for both parties. The world's top-ranked Wharton School course has been the most popular for 13 consecutive years, and this book shares these essential insights with readers.
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.
outline_en: Analyzing 52 Most Common Cognitive Errors and Traps in Daily Life
Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

video_title: S1 04 Cant Hurt Me 我 刀槍不入
description: 大衛．哥金斯的自傳，講述他如何從貧困、家暴、肥胖的童年，蛻變為海軍海豹突擊隊隊員與世界頂尖耐力賽運動員。他是史上唯一完成海豹部隊、陸軍遊騎兵與空軍戰術管制組三項精英訓練的人。書中提出「40%法則」——人通常只發揮了潛力的40%，並在每章末尾設置挑戰，鼓勵讀者突破自我極限。
author: 大衛·哥金斯
description_cn: 大衛．哥金斯的自傳，講述他如何從貧困、家暴、肥胖的童年，蛻變為海軍海豹突擊隊隊員與世界頂尖耐力賽運動員。他是史上唯一完成海豹部隊、陸軍遊騎兵與空軍戰術管制組三項精英訓練的人。書中提出「40%法則」——人通常只發揮了潛力的40%，並在每章末尾設置挑戰，鼓勵讀者突破自我極限。
description_en: The autobiography of David Goggins, detailing his transformation from a childhood of poverty, domestic violence, and obesity into a Navy SEAL and one of the world's top endurance athletes. He is the only person in history to complete the elite training of the Navy SEALs, Army Rangers, and Air Force Tactical Air Control Party. The book introduces the '40% Rule'—which suggests that we only tap into 40% of our true potential—and features challenges at the end of each chapter to push readers beyond their limits. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 作者苦難童年與加入海豹部隊的蛻變
揭秘突破大腦極限的 40% 科學法則
如何利用痛苦作為自我超越的燃料
建立鋼鐵心智與應對人生極限的挑戰
quotes: 大腦會在我們只發揮了 40% 潛力時就發出求救訊號，真正的強者懂得跨越這道虛假的心防。
苦難是最好的鍛鍊場，它能幫你建立起無堅不摧的鋼鐵心智。
quotes_en: The mind signals exhaustion when we've only tapped 40% of our potential. True strength lies in pushing past this illusion.
Suffering is the ultimate training ground; it helps you build an indestructible, steel-like mind.
outline_en: From a Tragic Childhood to Navy SEAL Transformation
Revealing the 40% Rule to Push Beyond Mental Limits
How to Use Pain as Fuel for Self-Transcendence
Building a Mind of Steel to Face Life's Ultimate Challenges

video_title: S1 05 Satir 的對話練習
description: 李崇建老師介紹薩提爾模式「冰山理論」的經典作品。本書教導讀者透過冰山對話來理解自己、連結他人、改善人際關係。薩提爾認為每個人都有可見的行為（冰山一角）和更深層的內在（感受、期待、渴望與自我），透過探索這些層次，能增進彼此理解、化解衝突，適用於親子、伴侶、師生等各種關係。
author: 李崇建
description_cn: 李崇建老師介紹薩提爾模式「冰山理論」的經典作品。本書教導讀者透過冰山對話來理解自己、連結他人、改善人際關係。薩提爾認為每個人都有可見的行為（冰山一角）和更深層的內在（感受、期待、渴望與自我），透過探索這些層次，能增進彼此理解、化解衝突，適用於親子、伴侶、師生等各種關係。
description_en: A classic work by Teacher Li Chong-jian introducing the 'Iceberg Theory' of the Satir Model. This book teaches readers how to understand themselves, connect with others, and improve interpersonal relationships through Iceberg dialogue. Satir posits that everyone has visible behaviors (the tip of the iceberg) and a deeper inner world (feelings, expectations, yearnings, and self). Exploring these layers fosters mutual understanding and resolves conflicts, making it applicable to parent-child, partner, and teacher-student relationships. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 認識薩提爾冰山理論的多層次結構
如何透過深度傾聽與同理心化解衝突
薩提爾對話在家庭與職場中的實務應用
探索內在渴望以實現與自我的溫柔連結
quotes: 每個人的行為只是冰山一角，真正決定我們關係的是冰山底下的感受、渴望與自我價值。
真正的對話不是為了說服，而是為了與對方的內在進行一場溫柔的連結。
quotes_en: Visible behavior is just the tip of the iceberg; what shapes relationships is the feelings, yearnings, and self-worth beneath.
True dialogue is not about persuasion, but about forging a gentle connection with another's inner self.
outline_en: Understanding the Layers of Satir's Iceberg Theory
Resolving Conflicts Through Deep Listening and Empathy
Practical Application of Satir's Dialogue in Families and Workplaces
Exploring Inner Yearnings for a Gentle Reconciliation with Self

video_title: S1 06 最高學習法
description: 日本精神科醫生樺澤紫苑的代表作，探討如何在日常工作與學習中，運用科學方法實現高效學習。書中結合腦科學與心理學研究，提出輸出導向的學習法，強調「輸入後必須輸出」才能真正記住知識，並提供提升專注力、記憶力與創造力的實用技巧。
author: 樺澤紫苑
description_cn: 日本精神科醫生樺澤紫苑的代表作，探討如何在日常工作與學習中，運用科學方法實現高效學習。書中結合腦科學與心理學研究，提出輸出導向的學習法，強調「輸入後必須輸出」才能真正記住知識，並提供提升專注力、記憶力與創造力的實用技巧。
description_en: The representative work of Japanese psychiatrist Shion Kabasawa, exploring how to apply scientific methods to achieve highly efficient learning in daily work and studies. Combining brain science and psychology research, the book proposes an output-oriented learning method, emphasizing that 'input must be followed by output' to truly retain knowledge, while offering practical techniques to boost focus, memory, and creativity. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 分析注意力流失的外部干擾與內部焦慮
設計無阻力的沉浸式深度工作環境
利用『誘因捆綁』與任務分解強化自律
養成完成任務的閉環思維與終局感
quotes: 專注是數位時代的稀缺資源。主動屏蔽雜訊，才能開啟深度工作。
完成你所開始的，是一項可以被刻意訓練的意志力技能。
quotes_en: Focus is a scarce resource in the digital age. Actively block noise to unlock deep work.
Finishing what you start is a willpower skill that can be deliberately trained.
outline_en: Analyzing External Distractions and Internal Attention Loss
Designing a Frictionless, Immersive Deep Work Environment
Strengthening Self-Discipline Through Temptation Bundling
Developing a Closed-Loop Mindset and a Sense of Completion

video_title: S1 07 成長型領導 Growth Leadership
description: 汪士瑋的領導力著作，探討如何透過成長型思維來提升團隊管理與領導效能。書中分享如何從固定型思維轉變為成長型領導模式，培養團隊成員的自主性與創新精神，建立持續進化的組織文化。
author: 汪士瑋
description_cn: 汪士瑋的領導力著作，探討如何透過成長型思維來提升團隊管理與領導效能。書中分享如何從固定型思維轉變為成長型領導模式，培養團隊成員的自主性與創新精神，建立持續進化的組織文化。
description_en: Written by Wang Shiwei, this is a practical guide for managers and leaders. Based on the consulting experience of over 3,500 managers, the book introduces 4 stages of growth-oriented leadership and 20+ practice diagrams. It emphasizes that solving people problems is key to solving performance problems, and builds a progressive leadership methodology through Taiwan-based case analysis and psychological techniques.
outline: 識別時間黑洞與拖延的深層根源
掌握高價值任務的優先排序法則
打造無阻力的高效專注執行系統
評估工作效能並優化每日能量分配
quotes: 時間不是被管理的，而是被選擇的。決定不做什麼，往往比決定做什麼更重要。
高效能的秘訣在於專注，而非盲目地將時間表填滿。
quotes_en: Time isn't managed; it is chosen. Deciding what not to do is often more critical than deciding what to do.
The secret of high productivity lies in focus, not blindly filling up your calendar.
outline_en: Identifying Time Sinks and Deep Roots of Procrastination
Mastering Priority Rules for High-Value Tasks
Building a Frictionless, High-Focus Execution System
Evaluating Work Efficiency and Optimizing Daily Energy Allocation

video_title: S1 08 目標 The Goal
description: 以色列物理學家高德拉特的經典商業小說，透過一個工廠經理挽救工廠的故事，介紹「限制理論」(TOC)。書中揭示瓶頸管理的核心原則：找出系統瓶頸、提升瓶頸產出、避免非瓶頸過度生產，最終實現整體效率最大化。被譽為最成功的企業管理寓言之一。
author: Eliyahu M. Goldratt
description_cn: 以色列物理學家高德拉特的經典商業小說，透過一個工廠經理挽救工廠的故事，介紹「限制理論」(TOC)。書中揭示瓶頸管理的核心原則：找出系統瓶頸、提升瓶頸產出、避免非瓶頸過度生產，最終實現整體效率最大化。被譽為最成功的企業管理寓言之一。
description_en: A classic business novel by Israeli physicist Eliyahu M. Goldratt that introduces the Theory of Constraints (TOC) through the story of a plant manager trying to save his factory. The book reveals the core principles of bottleneck management: identifying system bottlenecks, exploiting them, and avoiding overproduction at non-bottlenecks to ultimately maximize overall efficiency. It is widely acclaimed as one of the most successful business management allegories of all time. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 解構拖延心態背後的焦慮與完美主義
利用微小行動與『無計劃表』打破僵局
根據大腦能量狀態優化心智帶寬
建立可持續的抗拖延長效防禦機制
quotes: 克服拖延的最佳方式，就是立刻跨出第一步，即使是微不足道的行動。
心智管理大於時間管理，順應大腦的能量狀態安排工作才是關鍵。
quotes_en: The best way to beat procrastination is to take the first step immediately, no matter how trivial.
Mind management beats time management; arranging tasks based on brain energy is key.
outline_en: Deconstructing Anxiety and Perfectionism Behind Procrastination
Breaking Stalemates Using Micro-Actions and Unschedule
Optimizing Mental Bandwidth Based on Brain Energy States
Establishing Sustainable Long-Term Defenses Against Procrastination

title_cn: 為什麼事情做不完 你還在滑手機
video_title: S1 09 為什麼事情做不完 你還在滑手機
description: 舒婭探討現代人時間管理困境的實用指南。本書分析為何我們總是把時間浪費在手機上，卻完不成重要任務。透過心理學與行為科學的角度，提供克服拖延、建立專注力、管理數位干擾的具體方法，幫助讀者重新掌控時間與生活。
author: 舒婭
description_cn: 舒婭探討現代人時間管理困境的實用指南。本書分析為何我們總是把時間浪費在手機上，卻完不成重要任務。透過心理學與行為科學的角度，提供克服拖延、建立專注力、管理數位干擾的具體方法，幫助讀者重新掌控時間與生活。
description_en: This book provides an in-depth analysis of modern time management challenges. The author points out that the fundamental reason many people cannot complete tasks is often not lack of time, but distraction from smartphones and other interruptions. It offers practical strategies and techniques to help readers improve focus, manage time effectively, and overcome distracting habits.
outline: 分析注意力流失的外部干擾與內部焦慮
設計無阻力的沉浸式深度工作環境
利用『誘因捆綁』與任務分解強化自律
養成完成任務的閉環思維與終局感
quotes: 專注是數位時代的稀缺資源。主動屏蔽雜訊，才能開啟深度工作。
完成你所開始的，是一項可以被刻意訓練的意志力技能。
quotes_en: Focus is a scarce resource in the digital age. Actively block noise to unlock deep work.
Finishing what you start is a willpower skill that can be deliberately trained.
outline_en: Analyzing External Distractions and Internal Attention Loss
Designing a Frictionless, Immersive Deep Work Environment
Strengthening Self-Discipline Through Temptation Bundling
Developing a Closed-Loop Mindset and a Sense of Completion

title_en: Peak: Secrets from the New Science of Expertise
video_title: S1 10 刻意練習 Peak
description: 心理學家 Anders Ericsson 的經典著作，顛覆「一萬小時定律」的迷思。書中提出「刻意練習」的概念：練習不是簡單重複，而是需要有明確目標、即時回饋和持續挑戰舒適圈。從音樂家到運動員，揭示頂尖高手成功的真正秘密——不是天賦，而是正確的練習方法。
author: Anders Ericsson
description_cn: 心理學家 Anders Ericsson 的經典著作，顛覆「一萬小時定律」的迷思。書中提出「刻意練習」的概念：練習不是簡單重複，而是需要有明確目標、即時回饋和持續挑戰舒適圈。從音樂家到運動員，揭示頂尖高手成功的真正秘密——不是天賦，而是正確的練習方法。
description_en: This book introduces the power of deliberate practice. The author emphasizes that excellence is not the result of talent but is achieved through systematic, goal-oriented practice. It provides practical advice to help readers achieve outstanding performance in any field.
outline: 探索內在冰山結構以覺察自我情緒起點
掌握深度傾聽與同理心以化解人際衝突
薩提爾模式在親子、伴侶與關係中的應用
與內在脆弱和解以重建健全的自我價值
quotes: 溝通的本質不在於你說了什麼，而在於你是否聽懂了對方冰山底下的真實渴望。
接納不完美是與自己和解的第一步，也是療癒心靈焦慮的起點。
quotes_en: The essence of communication is not what you say, but whether you hear yearnings under the iceberg.
Accepting imperfection is the first step to reconciling with yourself and healing anxiety.
outline_en: Exploring the Iceberg Structure to Awaken the Origins of Self-Emotion
Mastering Deep Listening and Empathy to Resolve Interpersonal Conflicts
Applying Satir's Model in Parent-Child, Partner, and Relationship Scenarios
Reconciling with Inner Vulnerability to Rebuild Healthy Self-Worth

video_title: S1 11 剛剛好的孤獨 Solitude just right
description: 日本作家鎌田實探討現代人如何面對和享受孤獨的心靈之書。書中提倡「剛剛好的孤獨」——不是被動的孤立，而是主動打造屬於自己的獨處時光。透過挑戰新事物、接觸藝術、閱讀，培養獨立自處的能力，同時與他人保持無負擔的聯繫。
author: 鎌田實
description_cn: 日本作家鎌田實探討現代人如何面對和享受孤獨的心靈之書。書中提倡「剛剛好的孤獨」——不是被動的孤立，而是主動打造屬於自己的獨處時光。透過挑戰新事物、接觸藝術、閱讀，培養獨立自處的能力，同時與他人保持無負擔的聯繫。
description_en: This book explores the role and value of solitude in modern life. The author argues that moderate solitude is essential for personal growth and creativity, and not all loneliness is negative. It provides methods for finding balance in busy social lives and using solitude for self-reflection and creative thinking.
outline: 擺脫單一目標導向，建立持續的成長系統
利用『成長型思維』將犯錯轉化為大腦養分
解構自律肌肉的日常鍛鍊與能量補給
優化個人環境以減少自律的意志力損耗
quotes: 成功不是一蹴而就的事件，而是你每天堅持的小系統的產物。
挑戰舒適圈邊緣的難度，大腦的神經連結才會發生實質性改變。
quotes_en: Success is not a one-time event; it is the product of small systems you run daily.
Only by challenging the edge of your comfort zone will neural connections genuinely change.
outline_en: Moving Beyond Single Goals to Build Sustainable Growth Systems
Using Growth Mindset to Transform Mistakes into Brain Fuel
Deconstructing the Daily Training and Energy of Self-Discipline
Optimizing Personal Environment to Reduce Willpower Depletion

title_en: Positioning  The Battle for Your Mind
video_title: S1 12 定位 Positioning  The Battle for Your Mind
description: 行銷學經典《定位》，由 Al Ries 與 Jack Trout 合著。本書提出在資訊爆炸時代，行銷成功的關鍵在於搶佔消費者「心智中的位置」。強調品牌應建立簡單、清晰且獨特的定位，成為該品類的第一名，而非僅靠產品特性取勝。被譽為美國行銷史上最具影響力的概念之一。
author: 艾爾·里斯、傑克·特勞特
description_cn: 行銷學經典《定位》，由 Al Ries 與 Jack Trout 合著。本書提出在資訊爆炸時代，行銷成功的關鍵在於搶佔消費者「心智中的位置」。強調品牌應建立簡單、清晰且獨特的定位，成為該品類的第一名，而非僅靠產品特性取勝。被譽為美國行銷史上最具影響力的概念之一。
description_en: A marketing classic by Jack Trout. The book introduces the theory of 'positioning', emphasizing that in a competitive market, success depends on occupying a unique position in consumers' minds. Through numerous brand examples, the author demonstrates how effective positioning strategies build strong brand awareness and market advantage.
outline: 解構傳統對立談判的局限與情緒盲點
建立基於信任與價值創造的談判新框架
高難度商務溝通中的心理戰術與說服技巧
企業如何透過有效的制度與文化激發協作力
quotes: 談判不是非贏即輸的零和博弈，而是透過創造新價值來達成雙贏的合作。
理解對方的核心利益與潛在需求，是所有高難度溝通的決勝關鍵。
quotes_en: Negotiation is not a zero-sum game, but a collaborative partnership to create new value.
Understanding the opponent's core interests and underlying needs is key to high-stakes communication.
outline_en: Deconstructing Limits and Emotional Blindspots of Traditional Confrontational Negotiation
Establishing a New Negotiation Framework Based on Trust and Value Creation
Psychological Tactics and Persuasion Skills in High-Stakes Business Communication
How Enterprises Mobilize Collaborative Force Through Effective Culture

video_title: S1 13 時勢 Megatrends
description: 財經知識型 YouTuber 小Lin說的第一本商業金融科普書。本書深入分析日本、韓國、俄羅斯、英國、歐盟等國家的經濟發展歷程，揭示世界經濟運作的底層邏輯。透過活潑的比喻與豐富的資料圖表，讓讀者輕鬆理解景氣循環、國際關係與金融市場的脈動。
author: 小Lin說
description_cn: 財經知識型 YouTuber 小Lin說的第一本商業金融科普書。本書深入分析日本、韓國、俄羅斯、英國、歐盟等國家的經濟發展歷程，揭示世界經濟運作的底層邏輯。透過活潑的比喻與豐富的資料圖表，讓讀者輕鬆理解景氣循環、國際關係與金融市場的脈動。
description_en: The first popular business and finance book by financial YouTuber 'Xiao Lin Shuo.' This book deeply analyzes the economic development histories of Japan, South Korea, Russia, the UK, and the EU, revealing the underlying logic of how the global economy works. Through lively metaphors and rich data charts, it helps readers easily grasp business cycles, international relations, and the pulse of financial markets. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 識別人類大腦天生的心智盲區與思維慣性
建立跨學科多元思維模型以解構複雜問題
提升邏輯推導能力以過濾資訊時代的雜訊
升級思維操作系統以做出明智客觀的決策
quotes: 大腦的認知邊界，就是你人生的邊界。突破舊思維，才能看清局勢。
真正的智慧是學會像科學家一樣思考，隨時質疑自己的既有偏見。
quotes_en: The cognitive boundary of your mind is the boundary of your life. Break old mindsets to see clearly.
True wisdom is learning to think like a scientist, constantly questioning your own biases.
outline_en: Identifying Cognitive Blindspots and Thinking Inertia in the Human Brain
Establishing Multi-Disciplinary Mental Models to Deconstruct Complexity
Enhancing Logical Reasoning to Filter Noise in the Information Age
Upgrading the Mind's OS to Make Wise, Objective Decisions

video_title: S1 14 留白時間 Time Off
description: Juliet Funt 的時間管理著作，提倡在忙碌生活中創造「留白時間」。本書挑戰「忙碌等於生產力」的迷思，強調策略性暫停能提升創造力、專注力與決策品質。提供實用方法識別「時間小偷」（如完美主義與資訊超載），幫助讀者重新掌控節奏，以少做多。
author: Juliet Funt
description_cn: Juliet Funt 的時間管理著作，提倡在忙碌生活中創造「留白時間」。本書挑戰「忙碌等於生產力」的迷思，強調策略性暫停能提升創造力、專注力與決策品質。提供實用方法識別「時間小偷」（如完美主義與資訊超載），幫助讀者重新掌控節奏，以少做多。
description_en: This book emphasizes the importance of white space (rest time) in fast-paced modern life. The author argues that adequate rest and free time are crucial for physical, mental health, and creativity. It provides practical advice on creating white space in busy schedules for effective rest and recharging.
outline: 解構拖延心態背後的焦慮與完美主義
利用微小行動與『無計劃表』打破僵局
根據大腦能量狀態優化心智帶寬
建立可持續的抗拖延長效防禦機制
quotes: 克服拖延的最佳方式，就是立刻跨出第一步，即使是微不足道的行動。
心智管理大於時間管理，順應大腦的能量狀態安排工作才是關鍵。
quotes_en: The best way to beat procrastination is to take the first step immediately, no matter how trivial.
Mind management beats time management; arranging tasks based on brain energy is key.
outline_en: Deconstructing Anxiety and Perfectionism Behind Procrastination
Breaking Stalemates Using Micro-Actions and Unschedule
Optimizing Mental Bandwidth Based on Brain Energy States
Establishing Sustainable Long-Term Defenses Against Procrastination

video_title: S1 15 情緒靈敏力 Emotional Agility
description: 哈佛醫學院心理學家 Susan David 的重要著作。本書提出「情緒靈敏力」四步框架：勇敢面對情緒、從情緒中抽離、依循核心價值觀行動、持續微小調整前進。強調情緒不分好壞，而是寶貴的訊號，學會靈活回應情緒，才能在變化中保持韌性與成長。
author: Susan David
description_cn: 哈佛醫學院心理學家 Susan David 的重要著作。本書提出「情緒靈敏力」四步框架：勇敢面對情緒、從情緒中抽離、依循核心價值觀行動、持續微小調整前進。強調情緒不分好壞，而是寶貴的訊號，學會靈活回應情緒，才能在變化中保持韌性與成長。
description_en: This book introduces the concept of emotional agility, emphasizing the importance of understanding and managing emotions in complex relationships. The author provides practical techniques and methods to help readers improve emotional awareness, better handle emotional challenges, and improve interpersonal relationships.
outline: 解構自控力與意志力運作的生理與神經科學基礎
對抗短期誘惑、戰勝內心焦慮與壓力的實用工具
發揮內向者的獨特優勢以在喧囂世界中發揮影響力
重建身心能量系統以達成高產出的自我控管
quotes: 意志力不是天生的性格特質，而是像肌肉一樣可以被鍛鍊的生理能力。
真正的專注與平靜，來自於學會在喧囂的世界中尋找屬於自己的安靜力量。
quotes_en: Willpower is not an innate trait, but a physical capacity that can be trained like a muscle.
True focus and peace come from learning to find your quiet strength in a noisy world.
outline_en: Deconstructing the Physiological and Neuroscience Basis of Willpower
Practical Tools to Resist Short-Term Temptations and Conquer Anxiety
Unlocking the Unique Strengths of Introverts to Project Influence
Rebuilding the Mind-Body Energy System for High-Productivity Self-Control

video_title: S1 16 底層邏輯2 Underlaying Logic 2
author: 劉潤
description_cn: 知名商業顧問劉潤延續百萬暢銷書《底層邏輯》的全新力作！本書專注於拆解商業、職場與社會關係運作的核心規律，教你如何在瞬息萬變、資訊繁雜的時代中，透過掌握事情的底質來進行精準決策。書中提供實用的思維模型，幫助你從繁雜的表象中抽離，迅速看清底層框架。這是一本適合創業者、管理人員與職場精英的必讀讀物。立即點擊下方連結，聽書掌握核心的底層邏輯！
description_en: A powerful new work by renowned business consultant Liu Run, following his million-copy bestseller 'Underlying Logic'! This book focuses on deconstructing the core principles of business, workplace, and social dynamics, teaching you how to make precise decisions in an era of rapid change and information overload by mastering the essence of things. It provides practical mental models to help you step back from complex appearances and quickly see the underlying framework. A must-read for entrepreneurs, managers, and workplace professionals. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 識別時間黑洞與拖延的深層根源
掌握高價值任務的優先排序法則
打造無阻力的高效專注執行系統
評估工作效能並優化每日能量分配
quotes: 時間不是被管理的，而是被選擇的。決定不做什麼，往往比決定做什麼更重要。
高效能的秘訣在於專注，而非盲目地將時間表填滿。
quotes_en: Time isn't managed; it is chosen. Deciding what not to do is often more critical than deciding what to do.
The secret of high productivity lies in focus, not blindly filling up your calendar.
outline_en: Identifying Time Sinks and Deep Roots of Procrastination
Mastering Priority Rules for High-Value Tasks
Building a Frictionless, High-Focus Execution System
Evaluating Work Efficiency and Optimizing Daily Energy Allocation

video_title: S1 17 精準選股 7Nothing But Net
author: 陳喬泓
description_cn: 這本書提供了股票選擇的實用指南。作者介紹了如何透過基本面分析和技術分析相結合，精準選擇具有投資價值的股票。書中強調長期投資和風險管理的重要性，並提供了實用的選股策略和工具。
description_en: This book provides a practical guide to stock selection. The author introduces how to combine fundamental analysis and technical analysis to precisely select stocks with investment value. It emphasizes the importance of long-term investing and risk management, providing practical stock selection strategies and tools.
outline: 剖析好策略與流於口號的壞策略的本質區別
透過診斷與指導方針聚焦企業核心資源
設計商業護城河以抵禦市場的隨機波動
企業在轉承與接班實務中的風險管理
quotes: 好策略的核心在於聚焦資源，痛擊對手最脆弱、最關鍵的致命處。
企業的基業長青，取決於能否在危機前未雨綢繆地開啟第二增長曲線。
quotes_en: The core of a good strategy is focusing resources to strike the most vulnerable and critical point.
An enterprise's longevity depends on opening a second growth curve before crisis hits.
outline_en: Analyzing the Difference Between Good Strategies and Slogan-Based Bad Strategies
Focusing Core Resources Through System Diagnostics and Guidelines
Designing Business Moats to Protect Against Random Market Fluctuations
Risk Management and Succession Practices in Corporate Transitions

video_title: S1 18 軟技能
description: 由劉潤、李笑來、萬維綱、吳軍等30位各領域專家共同撰寫的職場升級指南。以30封信的形式，從底層邏輯、訓練科目、精英經驗與競爭力轉化四大板塊，系統闡述溝通、表達、社交、目標管理等軟技能的重要性。強調在AI時代，軟技能是最難被取代的個人核心競爭力。
author: 劉潤、李笑來、萬維綱、吳軍、劉擎、薛兆豐 等
description_cn: 由劉潤、李笑來、萬維綱、吳軍等30位各領域專家共同撰寫的職場升級指南。以30封信的形式，從底層邏輯、訓練科目、精英經驗與競爭力轉化四大板塊，系統闡述溝通、表達、社交、目標管理等軟技能的重要性。強調在AI時代，軟技能是最難被取代的個人核心競爭力。
description_en: A career upgrade guide co-authored by 30 experts across various fields, including Liu Run, Li Xiaolai, Wan Weigang, and Wu Jun. Written in the form of 30 letters, it systematically explains the importance of soft skills like communication, presentation, networking, and goal management through four sections: underlying logic, training courses, elite experiences, and competitiveness transformation. It emphasizes that in the AI era, soft skills are the most irreplaceable core personal competencies. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
author_en: Liu Run, Li Xiaolai, Wan Weigang, Wu Jun et al.
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.
outline_en: Analyzing 52 Most Common Cognitive Errors and Traps in Daily Life
Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

video_title: S1 19 散漫者的學習法
author: 佐佐木俊尚
description_cn: 這本書介紹了散漫者的學習方法，強調不同的人有不同的學習風格。作者認為對於注意力容易分散的人，應該採用符合其特點的學習策略，而非強行適應傳統的學習方法。書中提供了實用的技巧幫助散漫者提高學習效率。
description_en: This book introduces learning methods for scattered-minded people, emphasizing that different people have different learning styles. The author argues that for people with easily distracted attention, learning strategies should match their characteristics rather than forcing adaptation to traditional methods. It provides practical techniques to help scattered-minded people improve learning efficiency.
outline: 識別時間黑洞與拖延的深層根源
掌握高價值任務的優先排序法則
打造無阻力的高效專注執行系統
評估工作效能並優化每日能量分配
quotes: 時間不是被管理的，而是被選擇的。決定不做什麼，往往比決定做什麼更重要。
高效能的秘訣在於專注，而非盲目地將時間表填滿。
quotes_en: Time isn't managed; it is chosen. Deciding what not to do is often more critical than deciding what to do.
The secret of high productivity lies in focus, not blindly filling up your calendar.
outline_en: Identifying Time Sinks and Deep Roots of Procrastination
Mastering Priority Rules for High-Value Tasks
Building a Frictionless, High-Focus Execution System
Evaluating Work Efficiency and Optimizing Daily Energy Allocation

video_title: S1 20 你不欠任何人 You Don't Owe Anyone
author: Emily P. Freeman
description_cn: 這本書探討了現代人的心理健康和自我認知問題。作者強調每個人都不應該為了滿足他人期望而改變自己，應該建立健康的自我邊界。書中提供了實用的建議，幫助讀者學會說「不」，建立自尊和自信。
description_en: This book explores mental health and self-awareness issues in modern people. The author emphasizes that everyone should not change themselves to meet others' expectations, but should establish healthy personal boundaries. It provides practical advice to help readers learn to say 'no', build self-esteem and confidence.
outline: 剖析主觀認知與客觀事實之間的巨大鴻溝
學習天才的32個跨界思考策略與系統模型
如何利用逆向思維解開看似無解的兩難困境
建立動態反饋機制以修正個人的信念地圖
quotes: 我們看到的世界並非真實世界，而是大腦根據既有模型構建的幻覺。
多模型思維能讓你擁有超越常人的視角，在混亂中找到隱藏的秩序。
quotes_en: The world we see is not the real world, but a projection constructed by our mental models.
Multi-model thinking grants you unique perspectives to find hidden order in chaos.
outline_en: Analyzing the Deep Gap Between Subjective Perception and Objective Reality
Learning 32 Multi-Model Strategies and Systems Used by Geniuses
Using Inversion Thinking to Resolve Seemingly Impossible Dilemmas
Establishing Dynamic Feedback to Correct Personal Map of Beliefs

title_cn: 艱困時代的經濟學思考
video_title: S1 21 Nexus
author: 阿巴希·巴納吉、艾絲特·杜芙若
description_cn: 諾貝爾經濟學獎得主阿巴希·巴納吉與艾絲特·杜芙若的重磅巨著！面對移民、關稅、貧富差距與氣候變遷等當代最棘手的社會難題，兩位大師以嚴謹的實證研究與溫暖的人文關懷，挑戰傳統經濟學教條，提出真正能改善社會福利的解決方案。本書為大眾看清當前世界局勢提供了全新的思維框架，非常適合想在混亂時代看清趨勢的讀者。立即點擊下方連結，聆聽諾貝爾獎大師的精闢思考！
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 分析注意力流失的外部干擾與內部焦慮
設計無阻力的沉浸式深度工作環境
利用『誘因捆綁』與任務分解強化自律
養成完成任務的閉環思維與終局感
quotes: 專注是數位時代的稀缺資源。主動屏蔽雜訊，才能開啟深度工作。
完成你所開始的，是一項可以被刻意訓練的意志力技能。
quotes_en: Focus is a scarce resource in the digital age. Actively block noise to unlock deep work.
Finishing what you start is a willpower skill that can be deliberately trained.
outline_en: Analyzing External Distractions and Internal Attention Loss
Designing a Frictionless, Immersive Deep Work Environment
Strengthening Self-Discipline Through Temptation Bundling
Developing a Closed-Loop Mindset and a Sense of Completion

video_title: S1 22 哈佛寫作課 Telling True Stories
author: Steven Pinker
description_cn: 這本書將哈佛大學 150 年的寫作教學經驗濃縮成「O.R.E.O MAP」寫作法則。書中強調意見、理由、證明和提案的重要性，幫助讀者掌握 7 大關鍵寫作能力。透過這套方法，讀者可以像哈佛學生一樣進行有效的寫作，提升文章的說服力和價值。
description_en: This book condenses 150 years of Harvard University's writing instruction experience into the 'O.R.E.O MAP' writing framework. It emphasizes the importance of Opinion, Reason, Explanation/Example, and Offer/Proposal, helping readers master 7 key writing skills. Through this method, readers can write as effectively as Harvard students, boosting the persuasiveness and value of their work. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 分析個人習慣的底層神經運作機制
設計無摩擦的習慣建立與觸發迴路
如何利用身份認同驅動持久的行為改變
克服瓶頸期以實現個人能力的螺旋上升
quotes: 微小的習慣在時間的複利效應下，會積累出驚人的巨大轉變。
真正的自律不是自我壓抑，而是為了解鎖更長遠的自由與成就。
quotes_en: Tiny habits, compounded over time, accumulate into astonishingly massive transformations.
True self-discipline is not self-suppression, but the key to unlocking long-term freedom.
outline_en: Analyzing the Neurological Mechanics of Personal Habits
Designing Frictionless Loops for Habit Formation
Driving Long-Lasting Behavior Change via Identity Shift
Overcoming Plateaus to Achieve Spiral Personal Growth

video_title: S1 23 高效寫作
author: 鄭緯筌
description_cn: 這本書提供了高效寫作的實用指南。作者介紹了如何快速產出高質量的文章，強調清晰的思維和有效的表達方式。書中提供了實用的寫作技巧和框架，幫助讀者提高寫作效率，在短時間內完成優質的寫作作品。
description_en: This book provides a practical guide to efficient writing. The author introduces how to quickly produce high-quality articles, emphasizing clear thinking and effective expression. It offers practical writing techniques and frameworks to help readers improve writing efficiency and complete quality writing in a short time.
outline: 解構拖延心態背後的焦慮與完美主義
利用微小行動與『無計劃表』打破僵局
根據大腦能量狀態優化心智帶寬
建立可持續的抗拖延長效防禦機制
quotes: 克服拖延的最佳方式，就是立刻跨出第一步，即使是微不足道的行動。
心智管理大於時間管理，順應大腦的能量狀態安排工作才是關鍵。
quotes_en: The best way to beat procrastination is to take the first step immediately, no matter how trivial.
Mind management beats time management; arranging tasks based on brain energy is key.
outline_en: Deconstructing Anxiety and Perfectionism Behind Procrastination
Breaking Stalemates Using Micro-Actions and Unschedule
Optimizing Mental Bandwidth Based on Brain Energy States
Establishing Sustainable Long-Term Defenses Against Procrastination

title_cn: 寫作是最好得自我投資
video_title: S1 24 寫作是最好得自我投資
author: 陳立飛
description_cn: 由陳立飛撰寫，這本書強調寫作對個人品牌建設的重要性。作者認為寫作不僅是文學創作，更是打造個人品牌和實現複利人生的工具。書中介紹了如何透過寫作提升競爭力，並掌握 AI 時代的競爭優勢。
description_en: Written by Chen Lifei, this book emphasizes the importance of writing for personal brand building. The author argues that writing is not just literary creation but a tool for building personal brands and achieving compound growth. It introduces how to enhance competitiveness through writing and gain competitive advantage in the AI era.
outline: 解構終身學習大腦的可塑性與發展潛能
建立高效自主的獨學策略與知識內化步驟
利用機率思維在人生重大轉折處進行理性決策
重置生活指南以開啟自我突破的下半場人生
quotes: 最好的投資是投資自己的大腦。持續的自我疊代是抵禦不確定性的唯一武器。
主動為自己的人生下注，在隨機性中尋找前行的方向。
quotes_en: The best investment is in your mind. Continuous iteration is your shield against uncertainty.
Actively place bets on your life to navigate forward amidst randomness.
outline_en: Deconstructing Platicity and Potential of the Lifelong Learning Brain
Establishing Efficient Strategies for Self-Directed Learning
Using Probabilistic Thinking to Make Choices at Key Transitions
Resetting the Life Blueprint to Launch a Breakthrough Second Half

video_title: S1 25 可複製的溝通力
author: 樊登
description_cn: 這本書介紹了可複製的溝通力框架。作者認為有效的溝通不是天賦，而是可以透過系統方法習得的技能。書中提供了實用的溝通策略和技巧，幫助讀者在各種場景中進行有效溝通，建立更好的人際關係。
description_en: This book introduces a replicable communication framework. The author argues that effective communication is not a talent but a skill that can be acquired through systematic methods. It provides practical communication strategies and techniques to help readers communicate effectively in various scenarios and build better relationships.
outline: 市場局勢診斷與企業核心優勢分析
價值主張設計與客戶真實痛點精準定位
制定具備連貫性與高執行力的商業戰略
開創無競爭藍海市場以實現價值創新
quotes: 在紅海的價格戰中掙扎是平庸的選擇，卓越的企業專注於開創藍海與價值創新。
商業的本質不是賣產品，而是發現並解決客戶最真實、最隱晦的痛點。
quotes_en: Struggling in price wars of red oceans is mediocre; excellent enterprises focus on blue oceans.
The essence of business is not selling products, but solving customers' deepest pain points.
outline_en: Market Diagnostics and Core Business Strength Analysis
Value Proposition Design and Pinpoint Targeting of Customer Pain Points
Formulating Coherent and Actionable Business Strategies
Creating Uncontested Blue Oceans to Realize Value Innovation

video_title: S1 26 低風險創業 Low risk entrepreneurship
author: 樊登
description_cn: 這本書提供了低風險創業的實用指南。作者強調創業不必冒極高的風險，透過科學方法和驗證式學習，可以在降低風險的同時實現創業目標。書中介紹了如何在市場中快速找到正確方向，避免資源浪費。
description_en: This book offers a practical guide to low-risk entrepreneurship. The author emphasizes that starting a business doesn't require taking extreme risks. By using scientific methods and validated learning, entrepreneurs can achieve their goals while minimizing risk. The book introduces how to quickly find the right direction in the market and avoid wasting valuable resources. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 剖析好策略與流於口號的壞策略的本質區別
透過診斷與指導方針聚焦企業核心資源
設計商業護城河以抵禦市場的隨機波動
企業在轉承與接班實務中的風險管理
quotes: 好策略的核心在於聚焦資源，痛擊對手最脆弱、最關鍵的致命處。
企業的基業長青，取決於能否在危機前未雨綢繆地開啟第二增長曲線。
quotes_en: The core of a good strategy is focusing resources to strike the most vulnerable and critical point.
An enterprise's longevity depends on opening a second growth curve before crisis hits.
outline_en: Analyzing the Difference Between Good Strategies and Slogan-Based Bad Strategies
Focusing Core Resources Through System Diagnostics and Guidelines
Designing Business Moats to Protect Against Random Market Fluctuations
Risk Management and Succession Practices in Corporate Transitions

title_cn: 聖經教你的18個接班秘訣
video_title: S1 27 聖經教你的18個接班秘訣
author: 施以諾
description_cn: 施以諾教授的經典著作！本書將古老聖經中的宏大智慧與現代企業管理、傳承和接班規劃完美結合。作者整理出 18 個核心的接班秘訣，每個祕訣都結合了知名企業家與聖經人物的生命故事。這是一部能幫你凝聚團隊信念、平穩完成組織承傳與人才培育的實戰指南。立即點擊下方連結，聽書汲取施以諾教授的經營智慧！
description_en: A classic work by Professor Shih Yi-nuo! This book perfectly combines the ancient wisdom of the Bible with modern corporate management, succession, and inheritance planning. The author outlines 18 core secrets of succession, each illustrated by the life stories of famous entrepreneurs and biblical figures. This is a practical guide to help you build team alignment, smoothly navigate organizational succession, and nurture talent. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 解構傳統對立談判的局限與情緒盲點
建立基於信任與價值創造的談判新框架
高難度商務溝通中的心理戰術與說服技巧
企業如何透過有效的制度與文化激發協作力
quotes: 談判不是非贏即輸的零和博弈，而是透過創造新價值來達成雙贏的合作。
理解對方的核心利益與潛在需求，是所有高難度溝通的決勝關鍵。
quotes_en: Negotiation is not a zero-sum game, but a collaborative partnership to create new value.
Understanding the opponent's core interests and underlying needs is key to high-stakes communication.
outline_en: Deconstructing Limits and Emotional Blindspots of Traditional Confrontational Negotiation
Establishing a New Negotiation Framework Based on Trust and Value Creation
Psychological Tactics and Persuasion Skills in High-Stakes Business Communication
How Enterprises Mobilize Collaborative Force Through Effective Culture

video_title: S1 28 一如既往 Same as Ever
author: Morgan Housel
description_cn: 這本書探討了堅持和一致性在個人成長中的重要性。作者認為成功的關鍵不在於偶爾的努力，而在於「一如既往」的持續行動。書中提供了實用的方法，幫助讀者建立良好的習慣，並在長期堅持中實現目標。
description_en: This book explores the importance of consistency and persistence in personal growth. The author argues that the key to success is not occasional effort but consistent action 'as always'. It provides practical methods to help readers build good habits and achieve goals through long-term persistence.
outline: 分析個人習慣的底層神經運作機制
設計無摩擦的習慣建立與觸發迴路
如何利用身份認同驅動持久的行為改變
克服瓶頸期以實現個人能力的螺旋上升
quotes: 微小的習慣在時間的複利效應下，會積累出驚人的巨大轉變。
真正的自律不是自我壓抑，而是為了解鎖更長遠的自由與成就。
quotes_en: Tiny habits, compounded over time, accumulate into astonishingly massive transformations.
True self-discipline is not self-suppression, but the key to unlocking long-term freedom.
outline_en: Analyzing the Neurological Mechanics of Personal Habits
Designing Frictionless Loops for Habit Formation
Driving Long-Lasting Behavior Change via Identity Shift
Overcoming Plateaus to Achieve Spiral Personal Growth

title_cn: 丹尼爾斯博士跑步方程式
video_title: S1 29 丹尼爾斯博士跑步方程式
author: Jack Daniels
description_cn: 由丹尼爾斯博士撰寫的跑步科學指南。書中介紹了跑步訓練的科學方法和原理，幫助跑者理解如何透過系統的訓練提高成績。作者強調個性化訓練計劃的重要性，並提供了實用的訓練方程式。
description_en: A running science guide written by Dr. Daniels. The book introduces scientific methods and principles of running training, helping runners understand how to improve performance through systematic training. The author emphasizes the importance of personalized training plans and provides practical training equations.
outline: 剖析威脅現代人壽命的四大慢性病致命殺手
從運動、營養、睡眠到精神健康的多維長壽方案
探索心靈力量、安慰劑效應與身體治癒的科學證據
制定科學防老與提升晚年生命質量的實操指南
quotes: 健康的長壽不是單純延長壽命，而是要維持高品質的『健康壽命』。
心靈與身體絕非分離，我們的信念與情緒會深刻影響生理免疫系統。
quotes_en: Healthy longevity is not just extending lifespan, but sustaining a high-quality healthspan.
Mind and body are not separate; our beliefs and emotions profoundly shape our immune system.
outline_en: Analyzing the Four Chronic Killers Threatening Modern Lifespan
A Multi-Dimensional Longevity Plan Covering Exercise, Nutrition, and Sleep
Exploring Scientific Evidence of Mind Power and Self-Healing
Formulating Practical Guidelines for Healthy Aging and Lifespan Quality

video_title: S1 30 跑步教科書 Illustrated Guide for Runners
author: 中野．詹姆士．修一
description_cn: 這本書是跑步愛好者的教科書。作者介紹了跑步的基礎知識、訓練方法和常見問題的解決方案。書中強調科學訓練的重要性，並提供了詳細的訓練指南，幫助跑者安全有效地提高成績。
description_en: This book is a textbook for running enthusiasts. The author introduces basic running knowledge, training methods, and solutions to common problems. It emphasizes the importance of scientific training and provides detailed training guides to help runners safely and effectively improve their performance.
outline: 解構科學、物理學與人類知識進步的底層本源
窺探森林生態中樹木感知、情感與溝通的祕密生命
從宇宙、自然到微觀生物的跨學科科學視野拓展
培養對大自然的敬畏精神與科學理性的完美融合
quotes: 科學進步的本源在於持續創造『好的解釋』，並用懷疑的精神不斷證偽它。
大自然隱藏著無數精巧的連結，正如樹木之間透過地底網絡相互照顧。
quotes_en: The source of scientific progress lies in creating good explanations and testing them with skepticism.
Nature hides countless exquisite connections, as trees care for each other via underground networks.
outline_en: Deconstructing the Foundations of Scientific and Physical Knowledge Progress
Prying into the Secret Life of Trees: Perception, Emotion, and Cooperation
Expanding Cross-Disciplinary Perspectives from Cosmos to Microorganisms
Cultivating a Synthesis of Awe for Nature and Scientific Rationality

video_title: S1 31 改善體質深呼吸 Deep Breathing for Health
author: 美木良介
description_cn: 這本書介紹了深呼吸對身體健康的重要性。作者強調正確的呼吸方法可以改善體質，增強免疫力。書中提供了實用的深呼吸技巧和練習方法，幫助讀者透過呼吸改善健康狀況。
description_en: This book introduces the importance of deep breathing for physical health. The author emphasizes that correct breathing methods can improve physical condition and enhance immunity. It provides practical deep breathing techniques and practice methods to help readers improve health through breathing.
outline: 瑞·達利歐的個人生命歷程與思維演進
引領生活與個人成長的核心決策原則
打造極度透明與求真的企業管理原則
系統化思考與應對未知風險的決策框架
quotes: 痛苦加上反省等於進步。不要逃避痛苦，它是你取得認知升級的起點。
建立極度求真與極度透明的文化，能讓個人與團隊做出最客觀理性的決策。
quotes_en: Pain plus reflection equals progress. Do not avoid pain; it is the starting point of cognitive upgrade.
Establishing a culture of radical truth and radical transparency allows individuals and teams to make the most rational decisions.
outline_en: Ray Dalio's Life Journey and Evolution of Thinking
Core Decision-Making Principles for Life and Growth
Building a Culture of Radical Transparency and Truth in Management
Systems Thinking and Decision Frameworks to Handle Unknown Risks

title_cn: 像頂尖運動員一樣思考
video_title: S1 32 像頂尖運動員一樣思考 The Genius of Athletes
author: Jean François Ménard
description_cn: 這本書探討了頂尖運動員的思維方式。作者分析了成功運動員的心理素質和思維特點，幫助讀者理解如何培養冠軍心態。書中提供了實用的心理訓練方法，適用於體育和生活的各個領域。
description_en: This book explores the thinking patterns of top athletes. The author analyzes the psychological qualities and thinking characteristics of successful athletes, helping readers understand how to develop a champion mindset. It provides practical psychological training methods applicable to sports and various areas of life.
outline: 剖析主觀認知與客觀事實之間的巨大鴻溝
學習天才的32個跨界思考策略與系統模型
如何利用逆向思維解開看似無解的兩難困境
建立動態反饋機制以修正個人的信念地圖
quotes: 我們看到的世界並非真實世界，而是大腦根據既有模型構建的幻覺。
多模型思維能讓你擁有超越常人的視角，在混亂中找到隱藏的秩序。
quotes_en: The world we see is not the real world, but a projection constructed by our mental models.
Multi-model thinking grants you unique perspectives to find hidden order in chaos.
outline_en: Analyzing the Deep Gap Between Subjective Perception and Objective Reality
Learning 32 Multi-Model Strategies and Systems Used by Geniuses
Using Inversion Thinking to Resolve Seemingly Impossible Dilemmas
Establishing Dynamic Feedback to Correct Personal Map of Beliefs

title_cn: 財務自由之路 李笑來
video_title: S1 33 財務自由之路 李笑來
author: 李笑來
description_cn: 由李笑來撰寫的財務自由指南。書中介紹了實現財務自由的方法和原理，強調長期投資和複利的力量。作者提供了實用的財務規劃建議，幫助讀者建立財務自由的思維方式。
description_en: A financial freedom guide written by Li Xiaolai. The book introduces methods and principles for achieving financial freedom, emphasizing the power of long-term investing and compound interest. The author provides practical financial planning advice to help readers develop a financial freedom mindset.
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.
outline_en: Analyzing 52 Most Common Cognitive Errors and Traps in Daily Life
Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

description_cn: 這本書探討了價值的本質和創造。作者認為理解價值對於投資和商業決策至關重要。書中介紹了如何評估資產的真實價值，並透過價值投資實現財務目標。
description_en: This book explores the nature and creation of value. The author argues that understanding value is crucial for investment and business decisions. It introduces how to assess the true value of assets and achieve financial goals through value investing.

價值主張設計與客戶真實痛點精準定位
制定具備連貫性與高執行力的商業戰略
開創無競爭藍海市場以實現價值創新

商業的本質不是賣產品，而是發現並解決客戶最真實、最隱晦的痛點。

Value Proposition Design and Pinpoint Targeting of Customer Pain Points
Formulating Coherent and Actionable Business Strategies
Creating Uncontested Blue Oceans to Realize Value Innovation

author: 郝旭烈
description_cn: 《致富覺察：培養點石成金的財富腦》由財務專家郝旭烈撰寫，幫助讀者培養財富思維與覺察能力。透過實用的理財觀念與案例分析，引導讀者建立正確的金錢觀，培養點石成金的財富腦。
description_en: This book introduces the psychological foundation of wealth creation. The author emphasizes that wealth creation requires not only techniques but also correct mindset and beliefs. It provides practical advice to help readers change their perception of money and build a psychological foundation for wealth.

學習天才的32個跨界思考策略與系統模型
如何利用逆向思維解開看似無解的兩難困境

quotes: 我們看到的世界並非真實世界，而是大腦根據既有模型構建的幻覺。
多模型思維能讓你擁有超越常人的視角，在混亂中找到隱藏的秩序。

video_title: S1 36 索羅斯的投資哲學 George Soros' Philosophy of Investment
author: George Soros
description_cn: 這本書介紹了索羅斯的投資哲學和方法。作者分析了這位傳奇投資者的投資原理和決策過程，幫助讀者理解如何進行有效的投資。書中強調反身性理論和風險管理的重要性。
description_en: This book introduces Soros's investment philosophy and methods. The author analyzes the investment principles and decision-making processes of this legendary investor, helping readers understand how to invest effectively. It emphasizes the importance of reflexivity theory and risk management.
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.
outline_en: Analyzing 52 Most Common Cognitive Errors and Traps in Daily Life
Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

title_en: Principles  Crafting Your Life's Guiding Philosophy
video_title: S1 37 原則 Principles  Crafting Your Life's Guiding Philosophy
author: Ray Dalio
description_cn: 由瑞·達利歐撰寫的人生和工作原則指南。書中介紹了作者在投資和管理中總結的 500 多條原則，涵蓋決策、風險管理、團隊建設等方面。這些原則經過實踐驗證，可應用於各個領域。
description_en: A guide to life and work principles written by Ray Dalio. The book introduces over 500 principles the author has summarized from investing and management, covering decision-making, risk management, and team building. These principles have been tested in practice and can be applied to various fields.
outline: 市場局勢診斷與企業核心優勢分析
價值主張設計與客戶真實痛點精準定位
制定具備連貫性與高執行力的商業戰略
開創無競爭藍海市場以實現價值創新
quotes: 在紅海的價格戰中掙扎是平庸的選擇，卓越的企業專注於開創藍海與價值創新。
商業的本質不是賣產品，而是發現並解決客戶最真實、最隱晦的痛點。
quotes_en: Struggling in price wars of red oceans is mediocre; excellent enterprises focus on blue oceans.
The essence of business is not selling products, but solving customers' deepest pain points.
outline_en: Market Diagnostics and Core Business Strength Analysis
Value Proposition Design and Pinpoint Targeting of Customer Pain Points
Formulating Coherent and Actionable Business Strategies
Creating Uncontested Blue Oceans to Realize Value Innovation

title_cn: 連結：從石器時代到 AI 紀元
video_title: S1 38+ 窮查理的普通常識
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description_cn: 《人類三部曲》作者尤瓦爾·赫拉利全新鉅作！從石器時代的黏土板、印刷術到現代 AI 的資訊網絡，本書深刻剖析人類資訊網絡的歷史與陷阱。赫拉利指出，資訊並不等於真理，過度擴張的資訊網絡若缺乏自我糾錯機制，將會威脅民主與世界秩序。這是一本解讀人工智慧時代下人類未來命運的警世之書，適合想了解 AI 對社會衝擊的你。立即點擊下方連結，聆聽赫拉利對未來的終極思考！
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 擺脫單一目標導向，建立持續的成長系統
利用『成長型思維』將犯錯轉化為大腦養分
解構自律肌肉的日常鍛鍊與能量補給
優化個人環境以減少自律的意志力損耗
quotes: 成功不是一蹴而就的事件，而是你每天堅持的小系統的產物。
挑戰舒適圈邊緣的難度，大腦的神經連結才會發生實質性改變。
quotes_en: Success is not a one-time event; it is the product of small systems you run daily.
Only by challenging the edge of your comfort zone will neural connections genuinely change.
outline_en: Moving Beyond Single Goals to Build Sustainable Growth Systems
Using Growth Mindset to Transform Mistakes into Brain Fuel
Deconstructing the Daily Training and Energy of Self-Discipline
Optimizing Personal Environment to Reduce Willpower Depletion

title_en: Poor Richard's Almanack by BENJAMIN  FRANKLIN
video_title: S1 39 窮理查的普通常識  Poor Richard's Almanack by BENJAMIN  FRANKLIN
author: Charlie Munger
description_cn: 股神巴菲特最推崇的創業合夥人查理·蒙格的智慧集成！這本書被投資界與職場精英視為不可多得的圭臬經典，蒙格在書中深刻介紹他的『多元思維模型』，教你如何避免決策偏誤、用普通常識解讀股市波動與複雜人生，教你在生活與投資中避開愚蠢的陷阱。立即點擊下方連結，聽書開始學習蒙格傳奇人生的智慧結晶！
description_en: The compilation of wisdom from Charlie Munger, Warren Buffett's most esteemed business partner! Revered as an invaluable classic by the investment community and professional elites, this book deeply introduces Munger's 'Latticework of Mental Models,' teaching you how to avoid cognitive biases, interpret stock market volatility and life's complexities with common sense, and avoid foolish traps in life and investing. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!

如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統

Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences

author: William Nickels
description_cn: 這本書提供了商業基礎知識的全面介紹。作者涵蓋了經濟學、會計、市場營銷、管理等商業領域的核心概念。書中以簡明易懂的方式說明複雜的商業原理，適合初學者和想要提升商業素養的讀者。
description_en: This book provides a comprehensive introduction to business fundamentals. The author covers core concepts in business fields including economics, accounting, marketing, and management. It explains complex business principles in a clear and understandable way, suitable for beginners and readers wanting to improve their business knowledge.
outline: 市場局勢診斷與企業核心優勢分析
價值主張設計與客戶真實痛點精準定位
制定具備連貫性與高執行力的商業戰略
開創無競爭藍海市場以實現價值創新
quotes: 在紅海的價格戰中掙扎是平庸的選擇，卓越的企業專注於開創藍海與價值創新。
商業的本質不是賣產品，而是發現並解決客戶最真實、最隱晦的痛點。

title_en: Flow  Optimal Experiences and the Psychology of Happiness
video_title: S1 41 心流 Flow  Optimal Experiences and the Psychology of Happiness
author: Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi
description_cn: 由米哈里·契克森米哈賴撰寫的心理學經典。書中介紹了「心流」理論，即人們在完全投入某項活動時的最優體驗狀態。作者透過數十萬筆經驗取樣研究，闡述了進入心流狀態的條件，並探討如何在日常生活、工作和人際關係中達到心流。
description_en: A psychology classic written by Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi. The book introduces the 'flow' theory, describing the optimal experience state when people are completely immersed in an activity. Through hundreds of thousands of experience samples, the author explains the conditions for entering flow and explores how to achieve flow in daily life, work, and relationships.
outline: 識別情緒勒索陷阱並設立健康的心理邊界
分析內心對話（Chatter）對情緒與決策的操縱
透過科學方法轉化內心批判為積極動力
培養情緒韌性以應對外界無常的壓力與挑戰
quotes: 善良不是無底線的妥協，你的溫柔必須配上堅強的防禦與健康的邊界。
我們大腦的內心對話，既可以是摧毀自信的雜訊，也可以是引導行動的良師。
quotes_en: Kindness is not bottomless compromise; your warmth must pair with strong defenses and boundaries.
Our inner dialogue can either be a self-destructive noise or a wise mentor guiding action.
outline_en: Identifying Emotional Blackmail Traps and Establishing Healthy Boundaries
Analyzing How Inner Dialogue (Chatter) Manipulates Emotion and Decisions
Using Scientific Methods to Refine Inner Criticism into Positive Motivation
Cultivating Emotional Resilience to Face External Stress and Challenges

video_title: S1 42 學習究竟是什麼 New Science of Learning
author: 萬維鋼
description_cn: 《學習究竟是什麼》由「得到」App專欄作家萬維鋼撰寫，結合最新的心理學及腦科學研究成果，探討科學高效的學習方法。幫助讀者成為更有智慧的學習者，掌握學習的底層邏輯。
description_en: This book deeply explores the nature of learning. The author combines psychological and neuroscience perspectives with scientific research and observations of learning experts to summarize a systematic, scientific, and efficient learning theory. It provides practical learning advice to help readers become more efficient learners.
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統

author: Barbara Oakley
description_cn: 這本書介紹了如何學習的方法論。作者強調學習是一項可以習得的技能，而非天賦。書中提供了實用的學習策略和技巧，幫助讀者掌握有效的學習方法，提高學習效率和成果。
description_en: This book introduces the methodology of how to learn. The author emphasizes that learning is an acquirable skill rather than a talent. It provides practical learning strategies and techniques to help readers master effective learning methods and improve learning efficiency and outcomes.
outline: 識別時間黑洞與拖延的深層根源
掌握高價值任務的優先排序法則
打造無阻力的高效專注執行系統
評估工作效能並優化每日能量分配
quotes: 時間不是被管理的，而是被選擇的。決定不做什麼，往往比決定做什麼更重要。
高效能的秘訣在於專注，而非盲目地將時間表填滿。
quotes_en: Time isn't managed; it is chosen. Deciding what not to do is often more critical than deciding what to do.
The secret of high productivity lies in focus, not blindly filling up your calendar.
outline_en: Identifying Time Sinks and Deep Roots of Procrastination
Mastering Priority Rules for High-Value Tasks
Building a Frictionless, High-Focus Execution System
Evaluating Work Efficiency and Optimizing Daily Energy Allocation

video_title: S1 44 大人的學習法 Mature Learning
author: 和田秀樹
description_cn: 這本書針對成人學習提出了獨特的方法。作者認為成人學習應該建立於能有安心生活的收入之上，強調工作與學習的平衡。書中提供了 7 個習慣和知識框架，幫助成人透過學習實現人生逆轉。
description_en: This book proposes unique methods for adult learning. The author argues that adult learning should be built on income that allows for a secure life, emphasizing the balance between work and learning. It provides 7 habits and knowledge frameworks to help adults achieve life transformation through learning.
outline: 擺脫單一目標導向，建立持續的成長系統
利用『成長型思維』將犯錯轉化為大腦養分
解構自律肌肉的日常鍛鍊與能量補給
優化個人環境以減少自律的意志力損耗
quotes: 成功不是一蹴而就的事件，而是你每天堅持的小系統的產物。
挑戰舒適圈邊緣的難度，大腦的神經連結才會發生實質性改變。
quotes_en: Success is not a one-time event; it is the product of small systems you run daily.
Only by challenging the edge of your comfort zone will neural connections genuinely change.
outline_en: Moving Beyond Single Goals to Build Sustainable Growth Systems
Using Growth Mindset to Transform Mistakes into Brain Fuel
Deconstructing the Daily Training and Energy of Self-Discipline
Optimizing Personal Environment to Reduce Willpower Depletion

video_title: S1 45 大人的高效獨學法
author: 本山勝寬
description_cn: 學習專家本山勝寬的高效自主升級指南！在終身學習的時代，大人的學習不能再靠死記硬背。本書深入淺出地解鎖大腦最自然的記憶與吸收機制，教你如何利用零碎時間、建立高效獨學的日常節奏，在工作之餘快速考取證照或掌握第二專長。適合想打破時間限制、快速增值的職場人。立即點擊下方連結，聽書掌握最適合大人的獨學法！
description_en: A guide to efficient self-directed upgrade by learning expert Katsuhiro Motoyama! In the era of lifelong learning, adult education can no longer rely on rote memorization. This book explains the brain's most natural memory and absorption mechanisms in a simple way, teaching you how to utilize fragmented time, build a daily rhythm for efficient self-learning, and quickly obtain certifications or master a second skill outside of work. Ideal for working professionals looking to break time constraints and add value rapidly. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 分析注意力流失的外部干擾與內部焦慮
設計無阻力的沉浸式深度工作環境
利用『誘因捆綁』與任務分解強化自律
養成完成任務的閉環思維與終局感
quotes: 專注是數位時代的稀缺資源。主動屏蔽雜訊，才能開啟深度工作。
完成你所開始的，是一項可以被刻意訓練的意志力技能。
quotes_en: Focus is a scarce resource in the digital age. Actively block noise to unlock deep work.
Finishing what you start is a willpower skill that can be deliberately trained.
outline_en: Analyzing External Distractions and Internal Attention Loss
Designing a Frictionless, Immersive Deep Work Environment
Strengthening Self-Discipline Through Temptation Bundling
Developing a Closed-Loop Mindset and a Sense of Completion

video_title: S1 46 50個級學習法 50 Super Learning Methods
author: 柿內尚文
description_cn: 這本書介紹了 50 個高級學習法。作者系統地整理了各種先進的學習方法和技巧，幫助讀者突破學習瓶頸。書中強調個性化學習的重要性，提供了針對不同學習風格的建議。
description_en: This book introduces 50 advanced learning methods. The author systematically organizes various advanced learning techniques and strategies to help readers break through learning plateaus. It emphasizes the importance of personalized learning and provides recommendations for different learning styles.
outline: 分析個人習慣的底層神經運作機制
設計無摩擦的習慣建立與觸發迴路
如何利用身份認同驅動持久的行為改變
克服瓶頸期以實現個人能力的螺旋上升
quotes: 微小的習慣在時間的複利效應下，會積累出驚人的巨大轉變。
真正的自律不是自我壓抑，而是為了解鎖更長遠的自由與成就。
quotes_en: Tiny habits, compounded over time, accumulate into astonishingly massive transformations.
True self-discipline is not self-suppression, but the key to unlocking long-term freedom.
outline_en: Analyzing the Neurological Mechanics of Personal Habits
Designing Frictionless Loops for Habit Formation
Driving Long-Lasting Behavior Change via Identity Shift
Overcoming Plateaus to Achieve Spiral Personal Growth

video_title: S1 47 超強記憶學習法 Super Learning
author: Jim Kwik
description_cn: 這本書提供了超強記憶的實用技巧。作者介紹了如何透過科學方法提升記憶力，包括記憶宮殿、聯想法等技巧。書中強調記憶訓練的重要性，幫助讀者建立持久的記憶能力。
description_en: This book provides practical techniques for super memory. The author introduces how to improve memory through scientific methods, including techniques like memory palace and association. It emphasizes the importance of memory training and helps readers develop lasting memory abilities.
outline: 剖析威脅現代人壽命的四大慢性病致命殺手
從運動、營養、睡眠到精神健康的多維長壽方案
探索心靈力量、安慰劑效應與身體治癒的科學證據
制定科學防老與提升晚年生命質量的實操指南
quotes: 健康的長壽不是單純延長壽命，而是要維持高品質的『健康壽命』。
心靈與身體絕非分離，我們的信念與情緒會深刻影響生理免疫系統。
quotes_en: Healthy longevity is not just extending lifespan, but sustaining a high-quality healthspan.
Mind and body are not separate; our beliefs and emotions profoundly shape our immune system.
outline_en: Analyzing the Four Chronic Killers Threatening Modern Lifespan
A Multi-Dimensional Longevity Plan Covering Exercise, Nutrition, and Sleep
Exploring Scientific Evidence of Mind Power and Self-Healing
Formulating Practical Guidelines for Healthy Aging and Lifespan Quality

video_title: S1 48 記憶訓練 Memory Training
author: Jim Kwik
description_cn: 這本書系統介紹了記憶訓練的方法。作者透過科學研究和實踐經驗，提供了詳細的記憶訓練計劃。書中強調艾賓浩斯遺忘曲線的應用，幫助讀者透過間隔複習有效保留知識。
description_en: This book systematically introduces memory training methods. Combining scientific research and practical experience, the author provides a detailed memory training program. The book emphasizes the application of the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve, helping readers effectively retain knowledge through spaced repetition. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 解構科學、物理學與人類知識進步的底層本源
窺探森林生態中樹木感知、情感與溝通的祕密生命
從宇宙、自然到微觀生物的跨學科科學視野拓展
培養對大自然的敬畏精神與科學理性的完美融合
quotes: 科學進步的本源在於持續創造『好的解釋』，並用懷疑的精神不斷證偽它。
大自然隱藏著無數精巧的連結，正如樹木之間透過地底網絡相互照顧。
quotes_en: The source of scientific progress lies in creating good explanations and testing them with skepticism.
Nature hides countless exquisite connections, as trees care for each other via underground networks.
outline_en: Deconstructing the Foundations of Scientific and Physical Knowledge Progress
Prying into the Secret Life of Trees: Perception, Emotion, and Cooperation
Expanding Cross-Disciplinary Perspectives from Cosmos to Microorganisms
Cultivating a Synthesis of Awe for Nature and Scientific Rationality

video_title: S1 49 深度思考的技術 Mastering Deeper Thinking
author: 齋藤嘉則
description_cn: 這本書介紹了深度思考的技術和方法。作者強調深度思考對於解決複雜問題的重要性。書中提供了實用的思考框架和工具，幫助讀者培養批判性思維和創新思維能力。
description_en: This book introduces techniques and methods for deep thinking. The author emphasizes the importance of deep thinking for solving complex problems. It provides practical thinking frameworks and tools to help readers develop critical and innovative thinking abilities.
outline: 識別人類大腦天生的心智盲區與思維慣性
建立跨學科多元思維模型以解構複雜問題
提升邏輯推導能力以過濾資訊時代的雜訊
升級思維操作系統以做出明智客觀的決策
quotes: 大腦的認知邊界，就是你人生的邊界。突破舊思維，才能看清局勢。
真正的智慧是學會像科學家一樣思考，隨時質疑自己的既有偏見。
quotes_en: The cognitive boundary of your mind is the boundary of your life. Break old mindsets to see clearly.
True wisdom is learning to think like a scientist, constantly questioning your own biases.
outline_en: Identifying Cognitive Blindspots and Thinking Inertia in the Human Brain
Establishing Multi-Disciplinary Mental Models to Deconstruct Complexity
Enhancing Logical Reasoning to Filter Noise in the Information Age
Upgrading the Mind's OS to Make Wise, Objective Decisions

video_title: S1 50 1% 閱讀術 1% Reading
author: 柳根贊
description_cn: 這本書介紹了 1% 的閱讀術。作者強調透過每天 15 分鐘的閱讀，可以開始知識儲蓄規劃。書中將閱讀分成四個層次，幫助讀者逐步學會閱讀要領，提升理解力和人生價值。
description_en: This book introduces the 1% reading technique. The author emphasizes that through 15 minutes of daily reading, one can start knowledge savings planning. It divides reading into four levels, helping readers gradually master reading essentials and enhance comprehension and life value.
outline: 擺脫單一目標導向，建立持續的成長系統
利用『成長型思維』將犯錯轉化為大腦養分
解構自律肌肉的日常鍛鍊與能量補給
優化個人環境以減少自律的意志力損耗
quotes: 成功不是一蹴而就的事件，而是你每天堅持的小系統的產物。
挑戰舒適圈邊緣的難度，大腦的神經連結才會發生實質性改變。
quotes_en: Success is not a one-time event; it is the product of small systems you run daily.
Only by challenging the edge of your comfort zone will neural connections genuinely change.
outline_en: Moving Beyond Single Goals to Build Sustainable Growth Systems
Using Growth Mindset to Transform Mistakes into Brain Fuel
Deconstructing the Daily Training and Energy of Self-Discipline
Optimizing Personal Environment to Reduce Willpower Depletion

video_title: S1 51 閱讀力大全
author: 齋藤孝
description_cn: 日本明治大學教授、暢銷作家齋藤孝的閱讀心法結晶！在智慧型手機充斥、資訊碎片化的時代，閱讀能力成為個人核心的競爭力。本書系統化介紹如何從讀不懂到深讀、如何進行主題式閱讀，以及如何把書中的精華轉化為自身的養分與寫作輸出，助你建立起強大的個人知識庫。適合所有想要重拾閱讀樂趣、提升思維深度的人。立即點擊下方連結，聽書掌握最全面的閱讀策略！
description_en: The culmination of reading insights from Takashi Saito, a professor at Meiji University and bestselling author! In an era dominated by smartphones and fragmented information, reading ability has become a core personal competency. This book systematically introduces how to transition from struggling to read to deep reading, how to perform thematic reading, and how to convert book insights into personal nourishment and writing outputs, helping you build a robust personal knowledge base. Perfect for anyone wishing to rediscover the joy of reading and elevate their depth of thinking. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.
outline_en: Analyzing 52 Most Common Cognitive Errors and Traps in Daily Life
Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

title_cn: 雜訊時代的高效資料整理術
video_title: S1 52 雜訊時代的高效資料整理術
author: 岡野純
description_cn: 這本書介紹了在雜訊時代進行高效資料整理的術。作者強調資訊管理的重要性，提供了實用的整理方法和工具。書中幫助讀者在資訊爆炸的時代有效篩選和整理資訊。
description_en: This book introduces efficient data organization techniques in the noise age. The author emphasizes the importance of information management and provides practical organization methods and tools. It helps readers effectively filter and organize information in an age of information explosion.
outline: 市場局勢診斷與企業核心優勢分析
價值主張設計與客戶真實痛點精準定位
制定具備連貫性與高執行力的商業戰略
開創無競爭藍海市場以實現價值創新
quotes: 在紅海的價格戰中掙扎是平庸的選擇，卓越的企業專注於開創藍海與價值創新。
商業的本質不是賣產品，而是發現並解決客戶最真實、最隱晦的痛點。
quotes_en: Struggling in price wars of red oceans is mediocre; excellent enterprises focus on blue oceans.
The essence of business is not selling products, but solving customers' deepest pain points.
outline_en: Market Diagnostics and Core Business Strength Analysis
Value Proposition Design and Pinpoint Targeting of Customer Pain Points
Formulating Coherent and Actionable Business Strategies
Creating Uncontested Blue Oceans to Realize Value Innovation

title_cn: 資訊彙整術 - YouTube 頻道主及川幸久的情報收集和表達技巧
video_title: S1 53 資訊彙整術 - YouTube 頻道主及川幸久的情報收集和表達技巧
author: 及川幸久
description_cn: 由 YouTube 頻道主及川幸久撰寫。書中介紹了情報收集和表達技巧，強調如何透過系統方法進行資訊彙整。作者分享了自身作為內容創作者的經驗，提供了實用的資訊管理和表達策略。
description_en: Written by YouTube channel creator Yukihisa Oikawa. The book introduces information gathering and expression techniques, emphasizing how to systematically organize information. The author shares personal experience as a content creator and provides practical information management and expression strategies.
outline: 剖析好策略與流於口號的壞策略的本質區別
透過診斷與指導方針聚焦企業核心資源
設計商業護城河以抵禦市場的隨機波動
企業在轉承與接班實務中的風險管理
quotes: 好策略的核心在於聚焦資源，痛擊對手最脆弱、最關鍵的致命處。
企業的基業長青，取決於能否在危機前未雨綢繆地開啟第二增長曲線。
quotes_en: The core of a good strategy is focusing resources to strike the most vulnerable and critical point.
An enterprise's longevity depends on opening a second growth curve before crisis hits.
outline_en: Analyzing the Difference Between Good Strategies and Slogan-Based Bad Strategies
Focusing Core Resources Through System Diagnostics and Guidelines
Designing Business Moats to Protect Against Random Market Fluctuations
Risk Management and Succession Practices in Corporate Transitions

video_title: S1 54 讀懂一本書
author: 樊登
description_cn: 這本書教讀者如何讀懂一本書。作者介紹了深度閱讀的方法和技巧，幫助讀者提升閱讀理解能力。書中強調主動閱讀的重要性，提供了實用的筆記和反思方法。
description_en: This book teaches readers how to understand a book. The author introduces methods and techniques for deep reading, helping readers improve reading comprehension. It emphasizes the importance of active reading and provides practical note-taking and reflection methods.
outline: 解構終身學習大腦的可塑性與發展潛能
建立高效自主的獨學策略與知識內化步驟
利用機率思維在人生重大轉折處進行理性決策
重置生活指南以開啟自我突破的下半場人生
quotes: 最好的投資是投資自己的大腦。持續的自我疊代是抵禦不確定性的唯一武器。
主動為自己的人生下注，在隨機性中尋找前行的方向。
quotes_en: The best investment is in your mind. Continuous iteration is your shield against uncertainty.
Actively place bets on your life to navigate forward amidst randomness.
outline_en: Deconstructing Platicity and Potential of the Lifelong Learning Brain
Establishing Efficient Strategies for Self-Directed Learning
Using Probabilistic Thinking to Make Choices at Key Transitions
Resetting the Life Blueprint to Launch a Breakthrough Second Half

title_cn: 你的善良必須更有力量
video_title: S1 55 你的善良必須更有力量 Deep Kindness
author: 海苔熊
description_cn: 知名心理學作家海苔熊的溫柔自癒指南！善良不是無底線的妥協，你的溫柔必須配上堅強的防禦。海苔熊在書中結合豐富的諮商故事，教你如何識別情緒勒索、設立健康的心理邊界，並在愛護他人的同時，學會自我接納與心靈修護。非常適合常感到被情緒勒索、疲憊不堪的你。立即點擊下方連結，聽書開始學會溫柔且堅定地面對世界！
description_en: A gentle self-healing guide by well-known psychology writer Haitaixiong (Hsu Hao-yi)! Kindness is not about compromising without boundaries; your tenderness must be coupled with strong defenses. Combining numerous counseling stories, the book teaches you how to identify emotional blackmail, establish healthy psychological boundaries, and learn self-acceptance and emotional restoration while caring for others. It is highly recommended for those who often feel emotionally blackmailed and exhausted. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 探索內在冰山結構以覺察自我情緒起點
掌握深度傾聽與同理心以化解人際衝突
薩提爾模式在親子、伴侶與關係中的應用
與內在脆弱和解以重建健全的自我價值
quotes: 溝通的本質不在於你說了什麼，而在於你是否聽懂了對方冰山底下的真實渴望。
接納不完美是與自己和解的第一步，也是療癒心靈焦慮的起點。
quotes_en: The essence of communication is not what you say, but whether you hear yearnings under the iceberg.
Accepting imperfection is the first step to reconciling with yourself and healing anxiety.
outline_en: Exploring the Iceberg Structure to Awaken the Origins of Self-Emotion
Mastering Deep Listening and Empathy to Resolve Interpersonal Conflicts
Applying Satir's Model in Parent-Child, Partner, and Relationship Scenarios
Reconciling with Inner Vulnerability to Rebuild Healthy Self-Worth

title_en: Find Your True Voice  Healing Trauma and Finding Joy
video_title: S1 56 內心對話的力量 Find Your True Voice  Healing Trauma and Finding Joy
author: Ethan Kross
description_cn: 密西根大學心理學教授伊森·克羅斯的重磅代表作！我們的大腦裡每分每秒都充斥著內心對話，但這些聲音有時會變成嚴厲的批判與焦慮。本書教你如何跳出『自我內耗』的死胡同，學會用科學方法（如第三人稱視角、環境整理）轉化內心批判，將大腦雜訊化為支持行動的強大助力。立即點擊下方連結，聽書掌握與自己和解的心靈對話術！
description_en: A groundbreaking work by Ethan Kross, a psychology professor at the University of Michigan! Our brains are filled with inner monologue every second, but these voices can sometimes turn into harsh criticism and anxiety. This book teaches you how to break free from the dead end of 'inner friction' and learn scientific methods (such as third-person perspective and environmental organization) to transform inner criticism, turning brain chatter into a powerful force that supports action. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 剖析主觀認知與客觀事實之間的巨大鴻溝
學習天才的32個跨界思考策略與系統模型
如何利用逆向思維解開看似無解的兩難困境
建立動態反饋機制以修正個人的信念地圖
quotes: 我們看到的世界並非真實世界，而是大腦根據既有模型構建的幻覺。
多模型思維能讓你擁有超越常人的視角，在混亂中找到隱藏的秩序。
quotes_en: The world we see is not the real world, but a projection constructed by our mental models.
Multi-model thinking grants you unique perspectives to find hidden order in chaos.
outline_en: Analyzing the Deep Gap Between Subjective Perception and Objective Reality
Learning 32 Multi-Model Strategies and Systems Used by Geniuses
Using Inversion Thinking to Resolve Seemingly Impossible Dilemmas
Establishing Dynamic Feedback to Correct Personal Map of Beliefs

video_title: S1 57 樂觀者的遠見 The Optimist's Telescope  A Vision for the Future
author: Bina Venkataraman
description_cn: 由比娜·文卡塔拉曼撰寫。書中介紹了在莽撞決斷時代如何克服短視。作者強調前瞻思考的重要性，從個人、企業到社會層面進行分析。書中提供了科學驗證的決策方法和實踐技巧。
description_en: Written by Bina Venkataraman. The book introduces how to overcome short-sightedness in an age of reckless decision-making. The author emphasizes the importance of forward-thinking, analyzing from personal, corporate, and societal levels. It provides scientifically validated decision-making methods and practical techniques.
outline: 解構傳統對立談判的局限與情緒盲點
建立基於信任與價值創造的談判新框架
高難度商務溝通中的心理戰術與說服技巧
企業如何透過有效的制度與文化激發協作力
quotes: 談判不是非贏即輸的零和博弈，而是透過創造新價值來達成雙贏的合作。
理解對方的核心利益與潛在需求，是所有高難度溝通的決勝關鍵。
quotes_en: Negotiation is not a zero-sum game, but a collaborative partnership to create new value.
Understanding the opponent's core interests and underlying needs is key to high-stakes communication.
outline_en: Deconstructing Limits and Emotional Blindspots of Traditional Confrontational Negotiation
Establishing a New Negotiation Framework Based on Trust and Value Creation
Psychological Tactics and Persuasion Skills in High-Stakes Business Communication
How Enterprises Mobilize Collaborative Force Through Effective Culture

author: 林書煒
description_cn: 這本書介紹了如何做個有溫度的人。作者強調人際互動中的溫暖和真誠的重要性。書中提供了實用的溝通和人際技巧，幫助讀者建立更有意義的人際關係。
description_en: This book introduces how to be a warm-hearted person. The author emphasizes the importance of warmth and sincerity in interpersonal interactions. It provides practical communication and interpersonal skills to help readers build more meaningful relationships.

掌握深度傾聽與同理心以化解人際衝突

author: charles-munger
description_cn: 巴費特最信任的終身合夥人——查理·蒙格的智慧結晶！這是一本被投資界與職場人士奉為圭臬的永恆經典。蒙格在書中首度公開他的『多元思維模型』，結合心理學、物理學、生物學等多學科的視角，教你如何避免愚蠢的決策、建立正確的普通常識與投資心法。無論是生活、職場還是理財，這本書的智慧都能指引方向。立即點擊下方連結，開始聆聽蒙格傳奇人生的核心思維！

description_en: A neuroscience work written by Jill Taylor. The book introduces the brain's four personalities and emphasizes how to make different brain regions work together seamlessly. The author argues that we are emotional beings rather than thinking beings and can choose to become who we want. It provides practical BRAIN five-step methods.

建立跨學科多元思維模型以解構複雜問題
提升邏輯推導能力以過濾資訊時代的雜訊

description_en: This book debunks common misconceptions and pseudoscience about the brain. Based on the latest neuroscience research, the author explains how the brain truly works. The book emphasizes that learning does not require innate talent and provides scientifically validated learning methods to help readers change how they view their own brains. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!

video_title: S1 63 同步大腦思維的時間支配術 Your Time   Brain Sync
author: 鈴木祐
description_cn: 這本書介紹了同步大腦思維的時間管理術。作者強調時間管理不僅是效率問題，更涉及大腦的運作方式。書中提供了實用的時間支配技巧，幫助讀者透過理解大腦節律來優化時間使用。
description_en: This book introduces time management techniques that synchronize with brain thinking. The author emphasizes that time management is not just about efficiency but involves how the brain works. It provides practical time management techniques to help readers optimize time use by understanding brain rhythms.
outline: 分析注意力流失的外部干擾與內部焦慮
設計無阻力的沉浸式深度工作環境
利用『誘因捆綁』與任務分解強化自律
養成完成任務的閉環思維與終局感
quotes: 專注是數位時代的稀缺資源。主動屏蔽雜訊，才能開啟深度工作。
完成你所開始的，是一項可以被刻意訓練的意志力技能。
quotes_en: Focus is a scarce resource in the digital age. Actively block noise to unlock deep work.
Finishing what you start is a willpower skill that can be deliberately trained.
outline_en: Analyzing External Distractions and Internal Attention Loss
Designing a Frictionless, Immersive Deep Work Environment
Strengthening Self-Discipline Through Temptation Bundling
Developing a Closed-Loop Mindset and a Sense of Completion

title_cn: 我為何會這樣 - MBTI人格類型的16種性格密碼
video_title: S1 64 我為何會這樣 - MBTI人格類型的16種性格密碼
author: Isabel Briggs Myers
description_cn: 這本書介紹了 MBTI 人格類型的 16 種性格密碼。作者透過科學方法解釋了不同人格類型的特點和優勢。書中幫助讀者理解自己的人格類型，進而改善人際關係和職業選擇。
description_en: This book introduces the 16 personality codes of MBTI personality types. The author uses scientific methods to explain the characteristics and strengths of different personality types. It helps readers understand their personality type and improve relationships and career choices.
outline: 探索內在冰山結構以覺察自我情緒起點
掌握深度傾聽與同理心以化解人際衝突
薩提爾模式在親子、伴侶與關係中的應用
與內在脆弱和解以重建健全的自我價值
quotes: 溝通的本質不在於你說了什麼，而在於你是否聽懂了對方冰山底下的真實渴望。
接納不完美是與自己和解的第一步，也是療癒心靈焦慮的起點。
quotes_en: The essence of communication is not what you say, but whether you hear yearnings under the iceberg.
Accepting imperfection is the first step to reconciling with yourself and healing anxiety.
outline_en: Exploring the Iceberg Structure to Awaken the Origins of Self-Emotion
Mastering Deep Listening and Empathy to Resolve Interpersonal Conflicts
Applying Satir's Model in Parent-Child, Partner, and Relationship Scenarios
Reconciling with Inner Vulnerability to Rebuild Healthy Self-Worth

video_title: S1 65 拖延心理學 Taming Procrastination
author: Jane Burka, Lenora Yuen
description_cn: 這本書深入探討了拖延的心理根源。作者認為拖延不是懶惰，而是一種心理機制。書中提供了實用的克服拖延的方法，幫助讀者理解自己的拖延行為，並透過改變心態實現改變。
description_en: This book deeply explores the psychological roots of procrastination. The author argues that procrastination is not laziness but a psychological mechanism. It provides practical methods to overcome procrastination, helping readers understand their procrastination behavior and achieve change through mindset shifts.
outline: 解構拖延心態背後的焦慮與完美主義
利用微小行動與『無計劃表』打破僵局
根據大腦能量狀態優化心智帶寬
建立可持續的抗拖延長效防禦機制
quotes: 克服拖延的最佳方式，就是立刻跨出第一步，即使是微不足道的行動。
心智管理大於時間管理，順應大腦的能量狀態安排工作才是關鍵。
quotes_en: The best way to beat procrastination is to take the first step immediately, no matter how trivial.
Mind management beats time management; arranging tasks based on brain energy is key.
outline_en: Deconstructing Anxiety and Perfectionism Behind Procrastination
Breaking Stalemates Using Micro-Actions and Unschedule
Optimizing Mental Bandwidth Based on Brain Energy States
Establishing Sustainable Long-Term Defenses Against Procrastination

title_en: What Life Should Mean To You
video_title: S1 66 自卑與超越  What Life Should Mean To You
author: Alfred Adler
description_cn: 由阿德勒撰寫的個體心理學經典。書中介紹了自卑感如何驅使人類行動，以及如何透過超越自卑實現人生意義。作者強調童年經歷對人生的影響，提供了實用的心理建議。
description_en: A classic of individual psychology written by Adler. The book introduces how inferiority drives human action and how to achieve life meaning through overcoming inferiority. The author emphasizes the impact of childhood experiences on life and provides practical psychological advice.
outline: 解構自控力與意志力運作的生理與神經科學基礎
對抗短期誘惑、戰勝內心焦慮與壓力的實用工具
發揮內向者的獨特優勢以在喧囂世界中發揮影響力
重建身心能量系統以達成高產出的自我控管
quotes: 意志力不是天生的性格特質，而是像肌肉一樣可以被鍛鍊的生理能力。
真正的專注與平靜，來自於學會在喧囂的世界中尋找屬於自己的安靜力量。
quotes_en: Willpower is not an innate trait, but a physical capacity that can be trained like a muscle.
True focus and peace come from learning to find your quiet strength in a noisy world.
outline_en: Deconstructing the Physiological and Neuroscience Basis of Willpower
Practical Tools to Resist Short-Term Temptations and Conquer Anxiety
Unlocking the Unique Strengths of Introverts to Project Influence
Rebuilding the Mind-Body Energy System for High-Productivity Self-Control

video_title: S1 67 活著覺得麻煩的人 Avoidant Personality
author: 岡田尊司
description_cn: 這本書探討了現代人感到生活麻煩的心理原因。作者介紹了如何改變對生活的態度，從而減輕心理負擔。書中提供了實用的心理調適方法，幫助讀者找到生活的意義和快樂。
description_en: This book explores the psychological reasons why modern people find life troublesome. The author introduces how to change one's attitude toward life to reduce psychological burden. It provides practical psychological adjustment methods to help readers find meaning and happiness in life.
outline: 探索內在冰山結構以覺察自我情緒起點
掌握深度傾聽與同理心以化解人際衝突
薩提爾模式在親子、伴侶與關係中的應用
與內在脆弱和解以重建健全的自我價值

Accepting imperfection is the first step to reconciling with yourself and healing anxiety.

Mastering Deep Listening and Empathy to Resolve Interpersonal Conflicts
Applying Satir's Model in Parent-Child, Partner, and Relationship Scenarios
Reconciling with Inner Vulnerability to Rebuild Healthy Self-Worth

video_title: S1 68 慣性思維 How do we know Ourselves
author: Michael McQueen
description_cn: 危機管理專家麥可·麥克奎恩（Michael McQueen）直擊思維盲區的力作！為什麼我們總是習慣用舊方法解決新問題？本書深刻揭示了人類大腦的思維慣性如何阻礙企業創新與個人職涯發展，並提供突破固有框架的五個具體步驟，幫你快速重塑決策模型，在變局中洞察先機。立即點擊下方連結，開始聆聽這部終結僵化思維的破局之作！
description_en: This book introduces the dangers of habitual thinking. The author emphasizes how habitual thinking limits our thinking and innovation. It provides methods to break habitual thinking patterns, helping readers develop flexible thinking approaches.
outline: 剖析主觀認知與客觀事實之間的巨大鴻溝
學習天才的32個跨界思考策略與系統模型
如何利用逆向思維解開看似無解的兩難困境
建立動態反饋機制以修正個人的信念地圖
quotes: 我們看到的世界並非真實世界，而是大腦根據既有模型構建的幻覺。
多模型思維能讓你擁有超越常人的視角，在混亂中找到隱藏的秩序。
quotes_en: The world we see is not the real world, but a projection constructed by our mental models.
Multi-model thinking grants you unique perspectives to find hidden order in chaos.
outline_en: Analyzing the Deep Gap Between Subjective Perception and Objective Reality
Learning 32 Multi-Model Strategies and Systems Used by Geniuses
Using Inversion Thinking to Resolve Seemingly Impossible Dilemmas
Establishing Dynamic Feedback to Correct Personal Map of Beliefs

title_cn: 為什麼我們總是相信自己是對的
title_en: Why We're Always So Sure We're Right
video_title: S1 69 為什麼我們總是相信自己是對的 Why We're Always So Sure We're Right
author: Julia Galef
description_cn: 商業決策專家 Julia Galef 顛覆認知的思維代表作！作者提出『偵察兵思維』與『士兵思維』的對比，指出絕大多數人出於本能，總在捍衛自己的既有觀點，而真正的智者卻能像偵察兵一樣，不帶偏見地探索客觀事實。本書教你如何建立勇於承認錯誤的胸懷，提升投資與商業決策的精準度。立即點擊下方連結，聽書掌握偵察兵的清醒眼光！
description_en: A mind-bending work on thinking by business decision-making expert Julia Galef! The author contrasts 'scout mindset' with 'soldier mindset,' pointing out that most people instinctively defend their pre-existing beliefs, whereas truly wise individuals explore objective facts without bias like a scout. This book teaches you how to develop the humility to admit mistakes, improving the accuracy of your investment and business decisions. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.
outline_en: Analyzing 52 Most Common Cognitive Errors and Traps in Daily Life
Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

description_en: This book introduces how to re-engage in dialogue with people. The author emphasizes the importance of effective communication and provides practical dialogue techniques. It helps readers improve interpersonal communication and build deeper connections.

description_cn: 這本書介紹了有效決策的方法和原理。作者強調決策不是直覺，而是一個系統過程。書中提供了實用的決策框架，幫助讀者在複雜情況下做出更好的決策。
description_en: This book introduces methods and principles of effective decision-making. The author emphasizes that decision-making is not intuition but a systematic process. It provides practical decision-making frameworks to help readers make better decisions in complex situations.
