video_title: S1 73 工作原力 The IDEA Mindset  Figure Out What You Want From Work and How to Get It
author: Bruce Daisley
description_cn: 前 Twitter 副總裁 Bruce Daisley 的快樂工作與高效管理指南！你是否覺得每天上班都像在消耗靈魂？作者結合最新的管理學與行為研究，提出改善職場文化、激發團隊能量的實戰方案。教你如何減少無效會議、重拾專注，並在工作中找回掌控感與真正的樂趣，成倍提升工作滿意度。立即點擊下方連結，聽書開啟快樂工作的原動力！
description_en: A guide to happy working and efficient management by Bruce Daisley, former Vice President of Twitter! Do you feel like going to work every day is draining your soul? Combining the latest management science and behavioral research, the author proposes practical solutions to improve workplace culture and stimulate team energy. He teaches you how to reduce unproductive meetings, regain focus, and find a sense of control and true joy in your work, doubling your job satisfaction. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 識別時間黑洞與拖延的深層根源
掌握高價值任務的優先排序法則
打造無阻力的高效專注執行系統
評估工作效能並優化每日能量分配
quotes: 時間不是被管理的，而是被選擇的。決定不做什麼，往往比決定做什麼更重要。
高效能的秘訣在於專注，而非盲目地將時間表填滿。
quotes_en: Time isn't managed; it is chosen. Deciding what not to do is often more critical than deciding what to do.
The secret of high productivity lies in focus, not blindly filling up your calendar.
outline_en: Identifying Time Sinks and Deep Roots of Procrastination
Mastering Priority Rules for High-Value Tasks
Building a Frictionless, High-Focus Execution System
Evaluating Work Efficiency and Optimizing Daily Energy Allocation

description_en: This book introduces the importance of a winning perspective. The author emphasizes the key role of perspective shifts in problem-solving. It provides practical methods for shifting perspectives to help readers view problems from different angles.

建立高效自主的獨學策略與知識內化步驟

重置生活指南以開啟自我突破的下半場人生

主動為自己的人生下注，在隨機性中尋找前行的方向。
quotes_en: The best investment is in your mind. Continuous iteration is your shield against uncertainty.
Actively place bets on your life to navigate forward amidst randomness.
outline_en: Deconstructing Platicity and Potential of the Lifelong Learning Brain
Establishing Efficient Strategies for Self-Directed Learning
Using Probabilistic Thinking to Make Choices at Key Transitions
Resetting the Life Blueprint to Launch a Breakthrough Second Half

outline: 識別情緒勒索陷阱並設立健康的心理邊界
分析內心對話（Chatter）對情緒與決策的操縱
透過科學方法轉化內心批判為積極動力
培養情緒韌性以應對外界無常的壓力與挑戰
quotes: 善良不是無底線的妥協，你的溫柔必須配上堅強的防禦與健康的邊界。
我們大腦的內心對話，既可以是摧毀自信的雜訊，也可以是引導行動的良師。
quotes_en: Kindness is not bottomless compromise; your warmth must pair with strong defenses and boundaries.
Our inner dialogue can either be a self-destructive noise or a wise mentor guiding action.
outline_en: Identifying Emotional Blackmail Traps and Establishing Healthy Boundaries
Analyzing How Inner Dialogue (Chatter) Manipulates Emotion and Decisions
Using Scientific Methods to Refine Inner Criticism into Positive Motivation
Cultivating Emotional Resilience to Face External Stress and Challenges

title_cn: 精準回饋：提升團隊績效，改善溝通的超能⼒
video_title: S1 78 精準回饋：提升團隊績效，改善溝通的超能⼒
author: Joe Hirsch
description_cn: 這本書介紹了精準回饋的藝術。作者強調有效回饋對於提升團隊績效的重要性。書中提供了實用的回饋技巧，幫助讀者透過改善溝通提升團隊表現。
description_en: This book introduces the art of precise feedback. The author emphasizes the importance of effective feedback for improving team performance. It provides practical feedback techniques to help readers enhance team performance through improved communication.
outline: 解構自控力與意志力運作的生理與神經科學基礎
對抗短期誘惑、戰勝內心焦慮與壓力的實用工具
發揮內向者的獨特優勢以在喧囂世界中發揮影響力
重建身心能量系統以達成高產出的自我控管
quotes: 意志力不是天生的性格特質，而是像肌肉一樣可以被鍛鍊的生理能力。
真正的專注與平靜，來自於學會在喧囂的世界中尋找屬於自己的安靜力量。
quotes_en: Willpower is not an innate trait, but a physical capacity that can be trained like a muscle.
True focus and peace come from learning to find your quiet strength in a noisy world.
outline_en: Deconstructing the Physiological and Neuroscience Basis of Willpower
Practical Tools to Resist Short-Term Temptations and Conquer Anxiety
Unlocking the Unique Strengths of Introverts to Project Influence
Rebuilding the Mind-Body Energy System for High-Productivity Self-Control

video_title: S1 79 你有正確回答問題嗎 Answer Intelligence  Raising Your AQ
author: 哈爾・乾雷格森
description_cn: 頂尖組織溝通大師哈爾·乾雷格森的著作！本書提出了創新的『AQ（回答商數）』概念，指出在現代職場中，僅有問題是不夠的，如何給出高情商、具說服力且直擊本質的回答才是決策與溝通的關鍵。書中提供具體的對答框架，幫助你迅速判斷對方的提問動機，給出最能打動人心、促成行動的精準解答。特別推薦給主管與商務談判者，立即點擊下方連結聆聽，提升你的回答智慧！
description_en: This book teaches readers how to answer questions correctly. The author emphasizes the art of asking and answering questions, providing practical communication techniques. It helps readers improve expression and comprehension abilities.
outline: 市場局勢診斷與企業核心優勢分析
價值主張設計與客戶真實痛點精準定位
制定具備連貫性與高執行力的商業戰略
開創無競爭藍海市場以實現價值創新
quotes: 在紅海的價格戰中掙扎是平庸的選擇，卓越的企業專注於開創藍海與價值創新。
商業的本質不是賣產品，而是發現並解決客戶最真實、最隱晦的痛點。
quotes_en: Struggling in price wars of red oceans is mediocre; excellent enterprises focus on blue oceans.
The essence of business is not selling products, but solving customers' deepest pain points.
outline_en: Market Diagnostics and Core Business Strength Analysis
Value Proposition Design and Pinpoint Targeting of Customer Pain Points
Formulating Coherent and Actionable Business Strategies
Creating Uncontested Blue Oceans to Realize Value Innovation

title_cn: 你要如何衡量你的人生
title_en: How Will You Measure Your Life
video_title: S1 80 你要如何衡量你的人生 How Will You Measure Your Life
author: Clayton M. Christensen
description_cn: 由克里斯汀生撰寫的人生規劃經典。書中介紹了如何衡量人生的真正標準。作者透過破壞式創新理論分析人生決策，提供了實用的人生規劃方法，幫助讀者建立幸福人生。
description_en: A classic on life planning written by Christensen. The book introduces how to measure the true standards of life. The author analyzes life decisions through disruptive innovation theory and provides practical life planning methods to help readers build a happy life.
outline: 擺脫單一目標導向，建立持續的成長系統
利用『成長型思維』將犯錯轉化為大腦養分
解構自律肌肉的日常鍛鍊與能量補給
優化個人環境以減少自律的意志力損耗
quotes: 成功不是一蹴而就的事件，而是你每天堅持的小系統的產物。
挑戰舒適圈邊緣的難度，大腦的神經連結才會發生實質性改變。
quotes_en: Success is not a one-time event; it is the product of small systems you run daily.
Only by challenging the edge of your comfort zone will neural connections genuinely change.
outline_en: Moving Beyond Single Goals to Build Sustainable Growth Systems
Using Growth Mindset to Transform Mistakes into Brain Fuel
Deconstructing the Daily Training and Energy of Self-Discipline
Optimizing Personal Environment to Reduce Willpower Depletion

video_title: S1 81 快思慢想 Thinking Fast and Slowl
author: Daniel Kahneman
description_cn: 由諾貝爾經濟學獎得主丹尼爾·康納曼撰寫。書中介紹了人類思考的兩大系統：快速直覺的系統一和緩慢理性的系統二。作者透過心理學實驗揭露認知偏誤如何影響決策，幫助讀者理解自己的思維方式。
description_en: Written by Nobel Prize-winning economist Daniel Kahneman. The book introduces two systems of human thinking: fast intuitive System 1 and slow rational System 2. Through psychological experiments, the author reveals how cognitive biases affect decision-making, helping readers understand their thinking patterns.
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.
outline_en: Analyzing 52 Most Common Cognitive Errors and Traps in Daily Life
Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

video_title: S1 82 做自己的生命設計師 Designing Your Life
author: Bill Burnett, Dave Evans
description_cn: 由史丹佛教授比爾·柏內特和戴夫·埃文斯撰寫。書中介紹了如何透過設計思考創造有意義又快樂的人生。作者提供了實用的生涯規劃工具和方法，幫助讀者設計自己的人生。
description_en: Written by Stanford professors Bill Burnett and Dave Evans. The book introduces how to create a meaningful and happy life through design thinking. The authors provide practical career planning tools and methods to help readers design their own lives.
outline: 分析個人習慣的底層神經運作機制
設計無摩擦的習慣建立與觸發迴路
如何利用身份認同驅動持久的行為改變
克服瓶頸期以實現個人能力的螺旋上升
quotes: 微小的習慣在時間的複利效應下，會積累出驚人的巨大轉變。
真正的自律不是自我壓抑，而是為了解鎖更長遠的自由與成就。
quotes_en: Tiny habits, compounded over time, accumulate into astonishingly massive transformations.
True self-discipline is not self-suppression, but the key to unlocking long-term freedom.
outline_en: Analyzing the Neurological Mechanics of Personal Habits
Designing Frictionless Loops for Habit Formation
Driving Long-Lasting Behavior Change via Identity Shift
Overcoming Plateaus to Achieve Spiral Personal Growth

video_title: S1 83 慷慨的力量 The Power of Generosity
author: Adam Grant
description_cn: 這本書介紹了慷慨的力量。作者強調慷慨不僅是道德品質，更是一種能帶來個人和社會回報的行為。書中提供了實用的建議，幫助讀者理解慷慨的價值和影響。
description_en: This book introduces the power of generosity. The author emphasizes that generosity is not only a moral quality but also a behavior that brings personal and social rewards. It provides practical advice to help readers understand the value and impact of generosity.
outline: 擺脫單一目標導向，建立持續的成長系統
利用『成長型思維』將犯錯轉化為大腦養分
解構自律肌肉的日常鍛鍊與能量補給
優化個人環境以減少自律的意志力損耗
quotes: 成功不是一蹴而就的事件，而是你每天堅持的小系統的產物。
挑戰舒適圈邊緣的難度，大腦的神經連結才會發生實質性改變。
quotes_en: Success is not a one-time event; it is the product of small systems you run daily.
Only by challenging the edge of your comfort zone will neural connections genuinely change.
outline_en: Moving Beyond Single Goals to Build Sustainable Growth Systems
Using Growth Mindset to Transform Mistakes into Brain Fuel
Deconstructing the Daily Training and Energy of Self-Discipline
Optimizing Personal Environment to Reduce Willpower Depletion

title_en: Focus Project  A Year of Single Mindedness
video_title: S1 84 最有專注力的一年 Focus Project  A Year of Single Mindedness
author: Chris Bailey
description_cn: 這本書介紹了如何在一年內培養最高的專注力。作者強調專注力對於成功的重要性，提供了實用的方法幫助讀者改善專注力。書中包含了科學驗證的技巧和策略。
description_en: This book introduces how to develop peak focus in one year. The author emphasizes the importance of focus for success and provides practical methods to help readers improve their concentration. It includes scientifically validated techniques and strategies.
outline: 分析注意力流失的外部干擾與內部焦慮
設計無阻力的沉浸式深度工作環境
利用『誘因捆綁』與任務分解強化自律
養成完成任務的閉環思維與終局感
quotes: 專注是數位時代的稀缺資源。主動屏蔽雜訊，才能開啟深度工作。
完成你所開始的，是一項可以被刻意訓練的意志力技能。
quotes_en: Focus is a scarce resource in the digital age. Actively block noise to unlock deep work.
Finishing what you start is a willpower skill that can be deliberately trained.
outline_en: Analyzing External Distractions and Internal Attention Loss
Designing a Frictionless, Immersive Deep Work Environment
Strengthening Self-Discipline Through Temptation Bundling
Developing a Closed-Loop Mindset and a Sense of Completion

video_title: S1 85 淤泥效應 Sludge
author: Cass R. Sunstein
description_cn: 《淤泥效應：解開制度的束縛，重新找回組織執行力》由哈佛法學教授凱斯·桑思汀撰寫，揭示繁瑣的行政程序和制度障礙（即「淤泥」）如何阻礙人們完成重要事項。提出簡化制度、消除不必要阻力的方法，重新找回組織執行力。
description_en: This book introduces the sludge effect. The author emphasizes how to avoid falling into inefficient work states. It provides practical diagnostic and improvement methods to help readers escape busy but unproductive work patterns.
outline: 分析個人習慣的底層神經運作機制
設計無摩擦的習慣建立與觸發迴路
如何利用身份認同驅動持久的行為改變
克服瓶頸期以實現個人能力的螺旋上升
quotes: 微小的習慣在時間的複利效應下，會積累出驚人的巨大轉變。
真正的自律不是自我壓抑，而是為了解鎖更長遠的自由與成就。
quotes_en: Tiny habits, compounded over time, accumulate into astonishingly massive transformations.
True self-discipline is not self-suppression, but the key to unlocking long-term freedom.
outline_en: Analyzing the Neurological Mechanics of Personal Habits
Designing Frictionless Loops for Habit Formation
Driving Long-Lasting Behavior Change via Identity Shift
Overcoming Plateaus to Achieve Spiral Personal Growth

video_title: S1 86 最高精力管理法 Have More Energy
author: Tom Rath
description_cn: 這本書介紹了最高精力管理法。作者認為成功需要的不是時間管理，而是精力管理。書中提供了實用的方法，幫助讀者透過精力管理打造高效生活。
description_en: This book introduces the highest energy management method. The author argues that success requires energy management rather than time management. It provides practical methods to help readers build efficient lives through energy management.
outline: 解構拖延心態背後的焦慮與完美主義
利用微小行動與『無計劃表』打破僵局
根據大腦能量狀態優化心智帶寬
建立可持續的抗拖延長效防禦機制
quotes: 克服拖延的最佳方式，就是立刻跨出第一步，即使是微不足道的行動。
心智管理大於時間管理，順應大腦的能量狀態安排工作才是關鍵。
quotes_en: The best way to beat procrastination is to take the first step immediately, no matter how trivial.
Mind management beats time management; arranging tasks based on brain energy is key.
outline_en: Deconstructing Anxiety and Perfectionism Behind Procrastination
Breaking Stalemates Using Micro-Actions and Unschedule
Optimizing Mental Bandwidth Based on Brain Energy States
Establishing Sustainable Long-Term Defenses Against Procrastination

video_title: S1 87 科學休息 Rest  Efficient Recovery of Energy
author: Alex Soojung-Kim Pang
description_cn: 這本書介紹了科學休息的方法。作者強調休息不是懶惰，而是成長的必要條件。書中提供了實用的休息策略，幫助讀者透過正確的休息方式實現持久的精進。
description_en: This book introduces scientific rest methods. The author emphasizes that rest is not laziness but a necessary condition for growth. It provides practical rest strategies to help readers achieve sustained improvement through proper rest.
outline: 解構傳統對立談判的局限與情緒盲點
建立基於信任與價值創造的談判新框架
高難度商務溝通中的心理戰術與說服技巧
企業如何透過有效的制度與文化激發協作力
quotes: 談判不是非贏即輸的零和博弈，而是透過創造新價值來達成雙贏的合作。
理解對方的核心利益與潛在需求，是所有高難度溝通的決勝關鍵。
quotes_en: Negotiation is not a zero-sum game, but a collaborative partnership to create new value.
Understanding the opponent's core interests and underlying needs is key to high-stakes communication.
outline_en: Deconstructing Limits and Emotional Blindspots of Traditional Confrontational Negotiation
Establishing a New Negotiation Framework Based on Trust and Value Creation
Psychological Tactics and Persuasion Skills in High-Stakes Business Communication
How Enterprises Mobilize Collaborative Force Through Effective Culture

video_title: S1 88 創運思維 Lucky
author: 金度潤
description_cn: 《創運思維：滿手爛牌打到贏，解鎖致富、覆盤人生的七堂強運課》由韓國作家金度潤撰寫。透過七堂課揭示如何在不利條件下創造好運，翻轉人生。教導讀者運氣並非偶然，而是可以透過正確的思維和行動來創造。
description_en: This book introduces creative luck thinking. The author emphasizes that luck is not chance but an acquirable mindset. It provides practical methods to help readers create success through creative luck thinking.
outline: 識別人類大腦天生的心智盲區與思維慣性
建立跨學科多元思維模型以解構複雜問題
提升邏輯推導能力以過濾資訊時代的雜訊
升級思維操作系統以做出明智客觀的決策
quotes: 大腦的認知邊界，就是你人生的邊界。突破舊思維，才能看清局勢。
真正的智慧是學會像科學家一樣思考，隨時質疑自己的既有偏見。
quotes_en: The cognitive boundary of your mind is the boundary of your life. Break old mindsets to see clearly.
True wisdom is learning to think like a scientist, constantly questioning your own biases.
outline_en: Identifying Cognitive Blindspots and Thinking Inertia in the Human Brain
Establishing Multi-Disciplinary Mental Models to Deconstruct Complexity
Enhancing Logical Reasoning to Filter Noise in the Information Age
Upgrading the Mind's OS to Make Wise, Objective Decisions

video_title: S1 89 領導者該想什麼 Becoming a Technical Leader
author: Gerald M. Weinberg
description_cn: 這本書介紹了領導者應該思考的問題。作者強調系統思考和人際互動模型的重要性。書中提供了實用的領導力框架，幫助讀者成為真正解決問題的領導者。
description_en: This book introduces what leaders should think about. The author emphasizes the importance of systems thinking and interpersonal interaction models. It provides practical leadership frameworks to help readers become true problem-solving leaders.
outline: 剖析好策略與流於口號的壞策略的本質區別
透過診斷與指導方針聚焦企業核心資源
設計商業護城河以抵禦市場的隨機波動
企業在轉承與接班實務中的風險管理
quotes: 好策略的核心在於聚焦資源，痛擊對手最脆弱、最關鍵的致命處。
企業的基業長青，取決於能否在危機前未雨綢繆地開啟第二增長曲線。
quotes_en: The core of a good strategy is focusing resources to strike the most vulnerable and critical point.
An enterprise's longevity depends on opening a second growth curve before crisis hits.
outline_en: Analyzing the Difference Between Good Strategies and Slogan-Based Bad Strategies
Focusing Core Resources Through System Diagnostics and Guidelines
Designing Business Moats to Protect Against Random Market Fluctuations
Risk Management and Succession Practices in Corporate Transitions

title_en: Uncommon Wisdom to Inspire Your Life's Work
video_title: S1 90 你想成為什麼樣的人 Uncommon Wisdom to Inspire Your Life's Work
author: James Clear
description_cn: 這本書探討了你想成為什麼樣的人這個根本問題。作者強調自我認知和人生目標的重要性。書中提供了實用的自我探索方法，幫助讀者找到人生方向。
description_en: This book explores the fundamental question of what kind of person you want to become. The author emphasizes the importance of self-awareness and life goals. It provides practical self-exploration methods to help readers find their life direction.
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.
outline_en: Analyzing 52 Most Common Cognitive Errors and Traps in Daily Life
Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

video_title: S1 91 一切都是誘因的問題 The Why Axis
author: Uri Gneezy, John List
description_cn: 由葛尼奇和李斯特撰寫。書中介紹了誘因如何驅動人類行為。作者強調理解因果關係對於設計有效誘因的重要性。書中提供了實用的誘因設計方法。
description_en: Written by Uri Gneezy and John List, this book explains how incentives drive human behavior. The authors emphasize the importance of understanding causal relationships to design effective incentives. It provides practical methods for incentive design. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 探索內在冰山結構以覺察自我情緒起點
掌握深度傾聽與同理心以化解人際衝突
薩提爾模式在親子、伴侶與關係中的應用
與內在脆弱和解以重建健全的自我價值
quotes: 溝通的本質不在於你說了什麼，而在於你是否聽懂了對方冰山底下的真實渴望。
接納不完美是與自己和解的第一步，也是療癒心靈焦慮的起點。
quotes_en: The essence of communication is not what you say, but whether you hear yearnings under the iceberg.
Accepting imperfection is the first step to reconciling with yourself and healing anxiety.
outline_en: Exploring the Iceberg Structure to Awaken the Origins of Self-Emotion
Mastering Deep Listening and Empathy to Resolve Interpersonal Conflicts
Applying Satir's Model in Parent-Child, Partner, and Relationship Scenarios
Reconciling with Inner Vulnerability to Rebuild Healthy Self-Worth

video_title: S1 92 執行長日記 The Diary of a CEO
author: Steven Bartlett
description_cn: 由 Steven Bartlett 撰寫。書中介紹了 30 歲身價 40 億的成功心法。作者從創業、投資和訪談中提煉了 33 條人生法則，涵蓋知識、紀律和時間選擇。
description_en: Written by Steven Bartlett. The book introduces success principles from someone worth $4 billion at 30. The author distilled 33 life principles from entrepreneurship, investing, and interviews, covering knowledge, discipline, and time choices.
outline: 解構拖延心態背後的焦慮與完美主義
利用微小行動與『無計劃表』打破僵局
根據大腦能量狀態優化心智帶寬
建立可持續的抗拖延長效防禦機制
quotes: 克服拖延的最佳方式，就是立刻跨出第一步，即使是微不足道的行動。
心智管理大於時間管理，順應大腦的能量狀態安排工作才是關鍵。
quotes_en: The best way to beat procrastination is to take the first step immediately, no matter how trivial.
Mind management beats time management; arranging tasks based on brain energy is key.
outline_en: Deconstructing Anxiety and Perfectionism Behind Procrastination
Breaking Stalemates Using Micro-Actions and Unschedule
Optimizing Mental Bandwidth Based on Brain Energy States
Establishing Sustainable Long-Term Defenses Against Procrastination

author: Ashlee Vance
description_cn: 由 Ashlee Vance 撰寫的馬斯克傳記。書中介紹了馬斯克的成長歷程和創業經驗。作者透過詳細的故事和分析，揭示了這位天才企業家的思維方式和成功秘訣。

在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。

Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.

Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

video_title: S1 94 馬斯克傳   唯一不設限、全公開傳記 Walter Isaacson
author: Walter Isaacson

設計無摩擦的習慣建立與觸發迴路

克服瓶頸期以實現個人能力的螺旋上升
quotes: 微小的習慣在時間的複利效應下，會積累出驚人的巨大轉變。
真正的自律不是自我壓抑，而是為了解鎖更長遠的自由與成就。
quotes_en: Tiny habits, compounded over time, accumulate into astonishingly massive transformations.
True self-discipline is not self-suppression, but the key to unlocking long-term freedom.
outline_en: Analyzing the Neurological Mechanics of Personal Habits
Designing Frictionless Loops for Habit Formation
Driving Long-Lasting Behavior Change via Identity Shift
Overcoming Plateaus to Achieve Spiral Personal Growth

video_title: S1 95 張忠謀 自傳 Morris Chang Autobiography
author: 張忠謀
description_cn: 台灣半導體教父、台積電創辦人張忠謀親筆撰寫的傳奇史詩！從歷經戰亂的童年、在哈佛與麻省理工的求學時光，到在德州儀器展露頭角，最終返台創立台積電，張忠謀首度完整披露他數十年來的策略思維、人生感悟與商業經營智慧。這不僅是一本個人自傳，更是一部微型全球半導體產業發展史。適合所有追求卓越的職場人士與管理者，立即點擊下方連結，開始聆聽大師的智慧人生！
description_en: The legendary epic autobiography written by Morris Chang, the father of Taiwan's semiconductor industry and founder of TSMC! From a childhood marked by war, his studies at Harvard and MIT, to rising to prominence at Texas Instruments, and finally returning to Taiwan to establish TSMC, Morris Chang fully discloses his decades of strategic thinking, life reflections, and business management wisdom for the first time. This is not just a personal autobiography, but also a miniature history of the global semiconductor industry's development. Perfect for all professionals and managers striving for excellence. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 剖析主觀認知與客觀事實之間的巨大鴻溝
學習天才的32個跨界思考策略與系統模型
如何利用逆向思維解開看似無解的兩難困境
建立動態反饋機制以修正個人的信念地圖
quotes: 我們看到的世界並非真實世界，而是大腦根據既有模型構建的幻覺。
多模型思維能讓你擁有超越常人的視角，在混亂中找到隱藏的秩序。
quotes_en: The world we see is not the real world, but a projection constructed by our mental models.
Multi-model thinking grants you unique perspectives to find hidden order in chaos.
outline_en: Analyzing the Deep Gap Between Subjective Perception and Objective Reality
Learning 32 Multi-Model Strategies and Systems Used by Geniuses
Using Inversion Thinking to Resolve Seemingly Impossible Dilemmas
Establishing Dynamic Feedback to Correct Personal Map of Beliefs

quotes_en: The core of a good strategy is focusing resources to strike the most vulnerable and critical point.
An enterprise's longevity depends on opening a second growth curve before crisis hits.

quotes_en: Negotiation is not a zero-sum game, but a collaborative partnership to create new value.
Understanding the opponent's core interests and underlying needs is key to high-stakes communication.
outline_en: Deconstructing Limits and Emotional Blindspots of Traditional Confrontational Negotiation
Establishing a New Negotiation Framework Based on Trust and Value Creation
Psychological Tactics and Persuasion Skills in High-Stakes Business Communication

author: Angela Duckworth

description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。

我們大腦的內心對話，既可以是摧毀自信的雜訊，也可以是引導行動的良師。
quotes_en: Kindness is not bottomless compromise; your warmth must pair with strong defenses and boundaries.
Our inner dialogue can either be a self-destructive noise or a wise mentor guiding action.
outline_en: Identifying Emotional Blackmail Traps and Establishing Healthy Boundaries
Analyzing How Inner Dialogue (Chatter) Manipulates Emotion and Decisions
Using Scientific Methods to Refine Inner Criticism into Positive Motivation

author: Lisa Feldman Barrett
description_cn: 世界知名心理學家麗莎·費德曼·巴瑞特的觀點結晶！本書挑戰了傳統『情緒是本能反應』的認知，指出情緒其實是大腦根據過去經驗對當前身體感覺的『預測與建構』。這項變革性的理論能幫助你從源頭重新認識焦慮與壓力的本質，掌握對自身情緒與意識的控制權，實現更高層次的心理韌性。立即點擊下方連結，開始聆聽這本顛覆大腦神經科學的重量級著作！
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。

建立跨學科多元思維模型以解構複雜問題
提升邏輯推導能力以過濾資訊時代的雜訊

真正的智慧是學會像科學家一樣思考，隨時質疑自己的既有偏見。
quotes_en: The cognitive boundary of your mind is the boundary of your life. Break old mindsets to see clearly.
True wisdom is learning to think like a scientist, constantly questioning your own biases.
outline_en: Identifying Cognitive Blindspots and Thinking Inertia in the Human Brain
Establishing Multi-Disciplinary Mental Models to Deconstruct Complexity
Enhancing Logical Reasoning to Filter Noise in the Information Age

True focus and peace come from learning to find your quiet strength in a noisy world.

description_cn: Byron Katie的經典著作，介紹「轉念作業」（The Work）方法，透過四個簡單問題幫助讀者質疑並轉化造成痛苦的想法，找到內在的平靜與自由。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 分析個人習慣的底層神經運作機制
設計無摩擦的習慣建立與觸發迴路
如何利用身份認同驅動持久的行為改變
克服瓶頸期以實現個人能力的螺旋上升
quotes: 微小的習慣在時間的複利效應下，會積累出驚人的巨大轉變。
真正的自律不是自我壓抑，而是為了解鎖更長遠的自由與成就。
quotes_en: Tiny habits, compounded over time, accumulate into astonishingly massive transformations.
True self-discipline is not self-suppression, but the key to unlocking long-term freedom.
outline_en: Analyzing the Neurological Mechanics of Personal Habits
Designing Frictionless Loops for Habit Formation
Driving Long-Lasting Behavior Change via Identity Shift
Overcoming Plateaus to Achieve Spiral Personal Growth

video_title: S2 07 放棄的力量 Power of Giving Up
author: Seth Godin
description_cn: Seth Godin探討何時該堅持、何時該放棄的策略指南。書中提出「低谷」概念，幫助讀者辨別值得堅持的事物與應該放棄的死胡同，做出更明智的人生決策。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 剖析好策略與流於口號的壞策略的本質區別
透過診斷與指導方針聚焦企業核心資源
設計商業護城河以抵禦市場的隨機波動
企業在轉承與接班實務中的風險管理
quotes: 好策略的核心在於聚焦資源，痛擊對手最脆弱、最關鍵的致命處。
企業的基業長青，取決於能否在危機前未雨綢繆地開啟第二增長曲線。
quotes_en: The core of a good strategy is focusing resources to strike the most vulnerable and critical point.
An enterprise's longevity depends on opening a second growth curve before crisis hits.
outline_en: Analyzing the Difference Between Good Strategies and Slogan-Based Bad Strategies
Focusing Core Resources Through System Diagnostics and Guidelines
Designing Business Moats to Protect Against Random Market Fluctuations
Risk Management and Succession Practices in Corporate Transitions

video_title: S2 08 練習不想要 Practicing Non Wanting
author: 松浦彌太郎
description_cn: 松浦彌太郎探討減法生活的哲學，引導讀者學會放下不必要的欲望與執著。透過簡約生活的實踐，找到真正重要的事物，活出更自在的人生。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.
outline_en: Analyzing 52 Most Common Cognitive Errors and Traps in Daily Life
Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

video_title: S2 09 思考的法則 Rules of Thinking
author: Edward de Bono
description_cn: Edward de Bono的思考方法論著作，系統性地介紹各種思考工具與技巧。幫助讀者跳脫慣性思維，學會更有創造力和效率的思考方式。
description_en: Edward de Bono's classic work on thinking methodology, systematically introducing various cognitive tools and techniques. This book helps readers break free from routine thinking habits and learn more creative and efficient ways of thinking. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 識別時間黑洞與拖延的深層根源
掌握高價值任務的優先排序法則
打造無阻力的高效專注執行系統
評估工作效能並優化每日能量分配
quotes: 時間不是被管理的，而是被選擇的。決定不做什麼，往往比決定做什麼更重要。
高效能的秘訣在於專注，而非盲目地將時間表填滿。
quotes_en: Time isn't managed; it is chosen. Deciding what not to do is often more critical than deciding what to do.
The secret of high productivity lies in focus, not blindly filling up your calendar.
outline_en: Identifying Time Sinks and Deep Roots of Procrastination
Mastering Priority Rules for High-Value Tasks
Building a Frictionless, High-Focus Execution System
Evaluating Work Efficiency and Optimizing Daily Energy Allocation

video_title: S2 10 機緣力 The Serendipity Mindset
author: Christian Busch
description_cn: Christian Busch的研究著作，探討如何創造和把握「有意義的巧合」。書中揭示成功人士如何培養敏銳的觀察力，將看似隨機的事件轉化為人生機遇。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 剖析主觀認知與客觀事實之間的巨大鴻溝
學習天才的32個跨界思考策略與系統模型
如何利用逆向思維解開看似無解的兩難困境
建立動態反饋機制以修正個人的信念地圖
quotes: 我們看到的世界並非真實世界，而是大腦根據既有模型構建的幻覺。
多模型思維能讓你擁有超越常人的視角，在混亂中找到隱藏的秩序。
quotes_en: The world we see is not the real world, but a projection constructed by our mental models.
Multi-model thinking grants you unique perspectives to find hidden order in chaos.
outline_en: Analyzing the Deep Gap Between Subjective Perception and Objective Reality
Learning 32 Multi-Model Strategies and Systems Used by Geniuses
Using Inversion Thinking to Resolve Seemingly Impossible Dilemmas
Establishing Dynamic Feedback to Correct Personal Map of Beliefs

video_title: S2 11 原子習慣 Atomic Habits   James Clear
author: James Clear
description_cn: James Clear的暢銷經典，提出習慣養成四法則：讓提示顯而易見、讓習慣有吸引力、讓行動輕而易舉、讓獎賞令人滿足。強調每天進步1%，一年後將進步37倍的複利效應。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 解構終身學習大腦的可塑性與發展潛能
建立高效自主的獨學策略與知識內化步驟
利用機率思維在人生重大轉折處進行理性決策
重置生活指南以開啟自我突破的下半場人生
quotes: 最好的投資是投資自己的大腦。持續的自我疊代是抵禦不確定性的唯一武器。
主動為自己的人生下注，在隨機性中尋找前行的方向。
quotes_en: The best investment is in your mind. Continuous iteration is your shield against uncertainty.
Actively place bets on your life to navigate forward amidst randomness.
outline_en: Deconstructing Platicity and Potential of the Lifelong Learning Brain
Establishing Efficient Strategies for Self-Directed Learning
Using Probabilistic Thinking to Make Choices at Key Transitions
Resetting the Life Blueprint to Launch a Breakthrough Second Half

title_en: Seven Habits of Highly Effective People
video_title: S2 12 與成功有約 Seven Habits of Highly Effective People
author: Stephen R. Covey
description_cn: Stephen R. Covey的全球暢銷經典《高效能人士的七個習慣》，提出主動積極、以終為始、要事第一、雙贏思維、知彼解己、統合綜效、不斷更新七大原則，引導讀者從依賴走向獨立再到互賴。

author: Stephen R. Covey
description_cn: Stephen R. Covey繼《與成功有約》後的續作，探討如何從效率走向卓越。書中提出找到自己的「聲音」並幫助他人找到他們的聲音，實現從個人成功到組織成功的跨越。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 解構拖延心態背後的焦慮與完美主義
利用微小行動與『無計劃表』打破僵局
根據大腦能量狀態優化心智帶寬
建立可持續的抗拖延長效防禦機制
quotes: 克服拖延的最佳方式，就是立刻跨出第一步，即使是微不足道的行動。
心智管理大於時間管理，順應大腦的能量狀態安排工作才是關鍵。
quotes_en: The best way to beat procrastination is to take the first step immediately, no matter how trivial.
Mind management beats time management; arranging tasks based on brain energy is key.
outline_en: Deconstructing Anxiety and Perfectionism Behind Procrastination
Breaking Stalemates Using Micro-Actions and Unschedule
Optimizing Mental Bandwidth Based on Brain Energy States
Establishing Sustainable Long-Term Defenses Against Procrastination

author: Stephen R. Covey
description_cn: Stephen R. Covey將七個習慣的原則應用於婚姻關係的著作，探討如何在親密關係中實踐主動積極、雙贏思維等原則，建立更幸福、更持久的婚姻。

video_title: S2 15 我想跟你好好聊聊 Gonna Talk
author: 李松蔚
description_cn: 李松蔚的溝通指南，探討如何在關係中進行真誠、有效的對話。提供實用的溝通技巧，幫助讀者改善與家人、伴侶、朋友之間的關係品質。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 探索內在冰山結構以覺察自我情緒起點
掌握深度傾聽與同理心以化解人際衝突
薩提爾模式在親子、伴侶與關係中的應用
與內在脆弱和解以重建健全的自我價值
quotes: 溝通的本質不在於你說了什麼，而在於你是否聽懂了對方冰山底下的真實渴望。
接納不完美是與自己和解的第一步，也是療癒心靈焦慮的起點。
quotes_en: The essence of communication is not what you say, but whether you hear yearnings under the iceberg.
Accepting imperfection is the first step to reconciling with yourself and healing anxiety.
outline_en: Exploring the Iceberg Structure to Awaken the Origins of Self-Emotion
Mastering Deep Listening and Empathy to Resolve Interpersonal Conflicts
Applying Satir's Model in Parent-Child, Partner, and Relationship Scenarios
Reconciling with Inner Vulnerability to Rebuild Healthy Self-Worth

author: Simon Sinek
description_cn: Simon Sinek的暢銷著作，提出「黃金圈」理論：卓越的領導者從「為什麼」出發，而非「做什麼」。透過Apple、西南航空等案例，說明如何用使命感激勵人心，建立長期忠誠。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 解構傳統對立談判的局限與情緒盲點
建立基於信任與價值創造的談判新框架
高難度商務溝通中的心理戰術與說服技巧
企業如何透過有效的制度與文化激發協作力
quotes: 談判不是非贏即輸的零和博弈，而是透過創造新價值來達成雙贏的合作。
理解對方的核心利益與潛在需求，是所有高難度溝通的決勝關鍵。
quotes_en: Negotiation is not a zero-sum game, but a collaborative partnership to create new value.
Understanding the opponent's core interests and underlying needs is key to high-stakes communication.
outline_en: Deconstructing Limits and Emotional Blindspots of Traditional Confrontational Negotiation
Establishing a New Negotiation Framework Based on Trust and Value Creation
Psychological Tactics and Persuasion Skills in High-Stakes Business Communication
How Enterprises Mobilize Collaborative Force Through Effective Culture

author: Naval Ravikant
description_cn: Eric Jorgenson彙整矽谷投資人Naval Ravikant的智慧語錄與人生哲學。涵蓋財富創造、幸福追尋、哲學思考等主題，被譽為現代版的《窮查理的普通常識》。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。

建立跨學科多元思維模型以解構複雜問題
提升邏輯推導能力以過濾資訊時代的雜訊
升級思維操作系統以做出明智客觀的決策
quotes: 大腦的認知邊界，就是你人生的邊界。突破舊思維，才能看清局勢。
真正的智慧是學會像科學家一樣思考，隨時質疑自己的既有偏見。
quotes_en: The cognitive boundary of your mind is the boundary of your life. Break old mindsets to see clearly.
True wisdom is learning to think like a scientist, constantly questioning your own biases.
outline_en: Identifying Cognitive Blindspots and Thinking Inertia in the Human Brain
Establishing Multi-Disciplinary Mental Models to Deconstruct Complexity
Enhancing Logical Reasoning to Filter Noise in the Information Age
Upgrading the Mind's OS to Make Wise, Objective Decisions

description_en: This is a關於商業strategies和創業精神的practicalguide。The book分享了successful entrepreneurs的experience和lessons，為aspiringentrepreneursprovides了valuable的advice。

video_title: S2 21 The Unfair Advantage
author: Ash Ali, Hasan Kubba
description_cn: 席捲全球的創業與職場新思維指南！兩位資深創業者 Ash Ali 和 Hasan Kubba 指出，每個人都擁有屬於自己的『不公平優勢』，關鍵在於你是否能識別並善加利用。書中提出『MILES 框架』——從金錢、智力、地理位置、教育與地位等多個維度，教你如何發掘自身隱藏的獨特籌碼，在競爭中脫穎而出。適合想在職涯或創業中找到精準定位的你。立即點擊下方連結，聽書釋放你的不公平優勢！
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 識別人類大腦天生的心智盲區與思維慣性
建立跨學科多元思維模型以解構複雜問題
提升邏輯推導能力以過濾資訊時代的雜訊
升級思維操作系統以做出明智客觀的決策
quotes: 大腦的認知邊界，就是你人生的邊界。突破舊思維，才能看清局勢。
真正的智慧是學會像科學家一樣思考，隨時質疑自己的既有偏見。
quotes_en: The cognitive boundary of your mind is the boundary of your life. Break old mindsets to see clearly.
True wisdom is learning to think like a scientist, constantly questioning your own biases.
outline_en: Identifying Cognitive Blindspots and Thinking Inertia in the Human Brain
Establishing Multi-Disciplinary Mental Models to Deconstruct Complexity
Enhancing Logical Reasoning to Filter Noise in the Information Age
Upgrading the Mind's OS to Make Wise, Objective Decisions

video_title: S2 22 隱喻領導力 The Power of Leadership Metaphors
author: 陳朝益
description_cn: 華人頂尖教練陳朝益的領導力心法！在多元變動的商業時代，傳統的指令式管理已漸失效，如何發揮影響力凝聚團隊？本書介紹了『隱喻』這門強大的領導藝術，教你如何利用生動的比喻和故事，將抽象的企業願景、文化與價值觀直觀地植入團隊心中，激發成員的內在驅動力與共鳴。這是一本適合所有主管與團隊領袖的領導力讀物。立即點擊下方連結，聽書學習大師的領導藝術！
description_en: This is a關於workplace成功和職業發展的practicalguide。The bookcoversleadership、communication skills、teamwork等importanttopics，helpsprofessionals在highly competitive的workplace中stand out。
outline: 剖析好策略與流於口號的壞策略的本質區別
透過診斷與指導方針聚焦企業核心資源
設計商業護城河以抵禦市場的隨機波動
企業在轉承與接班實務中的風險管理
quotes: 好策略的核心在於聚焦資源，痛擊對手最脆弱、最關鍵的致命處。
企業的基業長青，取決於能否在危機前未雨綢繆地開啟第二增長曲線。
quotes_en: The core of a good strategy is focusing resources to strike the most vulnerable and critical point.
An enterprise's longevity depends on opening a second growth curve before crisis hits.
outline_en: Analyzing the Difference Between Good Strategies and Slogan-Based Bad Strategies
Focusing Core Resources Through System Diagnostics and Guidelines
Designing Business Moats to Protect Against Random Market Fluctuations
Risk Management and Succession Practices in Corporate Transitions

title_cn: 什麼都不教的上司才厲害
video_title: S2 23  什麼都不教的上司才厲害
author: 森泰造
description_cn: 日本管理學者森泰造的領導力著作，提出好的上司不應事事指導，而是創造讓部屬自主成長的環境。透過提問而非指令的方式，激發團隊的創造力與責任感。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 解構傳統對立談判的局限與情緒盲點
建立基於信任與價值創造的談判新框架
高難度商務溝通中的心理戰術與說服技巧
企業如何透過有效的制度與文化激發協作力
quotes: 談判不是非贏即輸的零和博弈，而是透過創造新價值來達成雙贏的合作。
理解對方的核心利益與潛在需求，是所有高難度溝通的決勝關鍵。
quotes_en: Negotiation is not a zero-sum game, but a collaborative partnership to create new value.
Understanding the opponent's core interests and underlying needs is key to high-stakes communication.
outline_en: Deconstructing Limits and Emotional Blindspots of Traditional Confrontational Negotiation
Establishing a New Negotiation Framework Based on Trust and Value Creation
Psychological Tactics and Persuasion Skills in High-Stakes Business Communication
How Enterprises Mobilize Collaborative Force Through Effective Culture

title_en: The Logic of Growth   Edmund Phelps
video_title: S2 26 增長的邏輯 The Logic of Growth   Edmund Phelps
author: 李善友
description_cn: 李善友的商業成長策略著作，系統性地分析企業增長背後的邏輯與規律。提供從產品、營銷到組織的全方位增長框架，幫助企業找到持續成長的動力。
description_en: A book on business growth strategy by Li Shanyou, which systematically analyzes the logic and patterns behind corporate growth. It provides a comprehensive growth framework spanning product, marketing, and organization, helping businesses find the momentum for sustainable growth. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.
outline_en: Analyzing 52 Most Common Cognitive Errors and Traps in Daily Life
Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

video_title: S2 27 下一個現在 The New Megatrends   Marian Salzman
author: Lee Smolin
description_cn: Lee Smolin探討時間本質的科學著作，挑戰傳統物理學對時間的理解。從量子力學到宇宙學，重新審視時間在物理世界中的角色與意義。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 識別時間黑洞與拖延的深層根源
掌握高價值任務的優先排序法則
打造無阻力的高效專注執行系統
評估工作效能並優化每日能量分配
quotes: 時間不是被管理的，而是被選擇的。決定不做什麼，往往比決定做什麼更重要。
高效能的秘訣在於專注，而非盲目地將時間表填滿。
quotes_en: Time isn't managed; it is chosen. Deciding what not to do is often more critical than deciding what to do.
The secret of high productivity lies in focus, not blindly filling up your calendar.
outline_en: Identifying Time Sinks and Deep Roots of Procrastination
Mastering Priority Rules for High-Value Tasks
Building a Frictionless, High-Focus Execution System
Evaluating Work Efficiency and Optimizing Daily Energy Allocation

title_en: AI世界的底層邏輯與生存法則
video_title: S2 28 AI世界的底層邏輯與生存法則
author: 劉潤
description_cn: 知名商業顧問劉潤專為 AI 時代讀者打造的生存與工作思維模型！在人工智慧技術飛速發展的今天，哪些工作會被取代？哪些能力將變得更加昂貴？劉潤帶領我們剝離技術的泡沫，直擊 AI 時代的本質規律：掌握不變的『底層邏輯』，理解社會協作、算法思維與個人價值的重新定義。這本書能幫你消除對未來的迷茫，找到精準的自我疊代路徑。立即點擊下方連結，聆聽 AI 時代的必備生存指南！
description_en: A survival and career thinking model specifically created for the AI era by renowned business consultant Run Liu! In today's rapidly developing world of artificial intelligence, which jobs will be replaced? Which skills will become more valuable? Run Liu strips away the tech bubble to directly address the essential laws of the AI era: mastering the unchanging 'underlying logic,' and understanding the redefinition of social collaboration, algorithmic thinking, and personal value. This book will help you eliminate confusion about the future and find a precise path for self-iteration. Click the link below to listen to this essential survival guide for the AI era!
outline: 剖析主觀認知與客觀事實之間的巨大鴻溝
學習天才的32個跨界思考策略與系統模型
如何利用逆向思維解開看似無解的兩難困境
建立動態反饋機制以修正個人的信念地圖
quotes: 我們看到的世界並非真實世界，而是大腦根據既有模型構建的幻覺。
多模型思維能讓你擁有超越常人的視角，在混亂中找到隱藏的秩序。
quotes_en: The world we see is not the real world, but a projection constructed by our mental models.
Multi-model thinking grants you unique perspectives to find hidden order in chaos.
outline_en: Analyzing the Deep Gap Between Subjective Perception and Objective Reality
Learning 32 Multi-Model Strategies and Systems Used by Geniuses
Using Inversion Thinking to Resolve Seemingly Impossible Dilemmas
Establishing Dynamic Feedback to Correct Personal Map of Beliefs

title_en: Sapiens  A Brief History of Humankind
video_title: S2 29 人類大歷史 Sapiens  A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description_cn: 全球暢銷百萬冊、跨世紀的歷史與科學奇書！歷史學家尤瓦爾·赫拉利以宏大的視角，帶領我們回顧智人如何從一種普通動物，憑藉『虛構故事的能力』進行大規模協作，最終躍升為地表主宰者。書中融合理性科學與人文思維，挑戰我們對帝國、金錢、宗教及科技發展的傳統看法。不論你想擴大自己的思維格局還是看清人類的未來，這都是必讀的經典。立即點擊下方連結，開始收聽這段波瀾壯闊的人類歷史！
description_en: This作品combines了人文和科學的perspective，為readersprovides了獨特的knowledgevision。throughinterdisciplinary的analysis，readers可以更comprehensive地understandworld。
outline: 認知革命：虛構故事如何讓智人開啟集體協作
農業革命：人類從採集者走向定居者的歷史陷阱
人類的融合：金錢、帝國與全球宗教的跨國界整合
科學革命：科技力量如何塑造現代帝國與人類未來
quotes: 人類之所以能稱霸地球，核心在於我們能共同相信虛構的故事並進行大規模協作。
認知革命、農業革命與科學革命，徹底改變了智人的命運與地球的生態格局。
quotes_en: Humans rule the Earth because we can believe in shared myths and cooperate on a massive scale.
The Cognitive, Agricultural, and Scientific Revolutions completely altered Homo Sapiens' destiny and global ecology.
outline_en: The Cognitive Revolution: How Shared Myths Enabled Collective Cooperation
The Agricultural Revolution: The Historical Trap of Transition to Settlement
The Unification of Humankind: Integration of Money, Empires, and Religions
The Scientific Revolution: How Tech Shapes Modern Empires and the Future

video_title: S2 31 你就是困住自己的那座山 The Montain is You   Brianna Wiest
author: Brianna Wiest
description_cn: Brianna Wiest的暢銷自我成長著作，探討我們如何成為自己最大的障礙。揭示自我破壞的模式，提供突破內在限制的實用方法，幫助讀者停止阻擋自己走向更好的人生。

優化個人環境以減少自律的意志力損耗

挑戰舒適圈邊緣的難度，大腦的神經連結才會發生實質性改變。

Only by challenging the edge of your comfort zone will neural connections genuinely change.

Using Growth Mindset to Transform Mistakes into Brain Fuel
Deconstructing the Daily Training and Energy of Self-Discipline
Optimizing Personal Environment to Reduce Willpower Depletion

主動為自己的人生下注，在隨機性中尋找前行的方向。

Actively place bets on your life to navigate forward amidst randomness.

Using Probabilistic Thinking to Make Choices at Key Transitions

title_cn: 大谷翔平   棒球雙刀流現象級傳奇
title_en: Shohei Ohtani Baseball's Two Way Legend
video_title: S2 33 大谷翔平   棒球雙刀流現象級傳奇 Shohei Ohtani Baseball's Two Way Legend
author: Jay Paris
description_cn: 棒球大師大谷翔平的現象級傳奇自傳！他如何打破百年棒球史，以無與倫比的『投打雙刀流』稱霸大聯盟？本書詳細記錄了大谷翔平在球場背後極致自律的鍛鍊心法、目標達成法（如著名的九宮格目標卡），以及面對壓力和傷痛時的堅定心態。適合所有想學習如何將目標化為現實的職場人士。立即點擊下方連結，聽書聆聽傳奇巨星的拼搏智慧！
description_en: 《大谷翔平   棒球雙刀流現象級傳奇》是一部深具inspiration性的work。This bookthroughin an accessible manner的方式，為readers呈現了important的knowledge和insights，值得carefully閱讀。
outline: 分析個人習慣的底層神經運作機制
設計無摩擦的習慣建立與觸發迴路
如何利用身份認同驅動持久的行為改變
克服瓶頸期以實現個人能力的螺旋上升
quotes: 微小的習慣在時間的複利效應下，會積累出驚人的巨大轉變。
真正的自律不是自我壓抑，而是為了解鎖更長遠的自由與成就。
quotes_en: Tiny habits, compounded over time, accumulate into astonishingly massive transformations.
True self-discipline is not self-suppression, but the key to unlocking long-term freedom.

Designing Frictionless Loops for Habit Formation
Driving Long-Lasting Behavior Change via Identity Shift
Overcoming Plateaus to Achieve Spiral Personal Growth

description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。

心智管理大於時間管理，順應大腦的能量狀態安排工作才是關鍵。

description_cn: 萬維鋼的科學思維著作，融合心理學、認知科學與哲學，幫助讀者升級思維作業系統。探討如何擺脫偏見、建立理性思考模式，在資訊時代做出更好的判斷。
description_en: A scientific thinking guide by Wengewang, integrating psychology, cognitive science, and philosophy to help readers upgrade their cognitive operating systems. It explores how to break free from biases, establish rational thinking models, and make better decisions in the information age. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!

完成你所開始的，是一項可以被刻意訓練的意志力技能。

description_en: 《不服從的藝術》是一部深具inspiration性的work。This bookthroughin an accessible manner的方式，為readers呈現了important的knowledge和insights，值得carefully閱讀。

description_cn: 零售商與市場營銷人員的聖經，行銷大師 Paco Underhill 的心法結晶！本書深入剖析消費者的購物心理學與購物習慣，結合對商場、超市等實體零售空間的大量觀察研究，揭示了哪些擺設能激發消費慾望、哪些細節會讓顧客卻步。這不僅是一本揭示商家套路的有趣讀物，更是商務人士設計產品、優化服務與提升成交率的實戰手冊。立即點擊下方連結，聆聽並解碼消費背後的行為科學！
description_en: This作品combines了人文和科學的perspective，為readersprovides了獨特的knowledgevision。throughinterdisciplinary的analysis，readers可以更comprehensive地understandworld。

價值主張設計與客戶真實痛點精準定位

開創無競爭藍海市場以實現價值創新

Through gentle herbal meals and adjustments to lifestyle thinking, it regulates the constitution from its roots to reshape physical and mental harmony. Highly recommended for stressed modern office workers. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!

設計無摩擦的習慣建立與觸發迴路
如何利用身份認同驅動持久的行為改變
克服瓶頸期以實現個人能力的螺旋上升
quotes: 微小的習慣在時間的複利效應下，會積累出驚人的巨大轉變。
真正的自律不是自我壓抑，而是為了解鎖更長遠的自由與成就。
quotes_en: Tiny habits, compounded over time, accumulate into astonishingly massive transformations.
True self-discipline is not self-suppression, but the key to unlocking long-term freedom.
outline_en: Analyzing the Neurological Mechanics of Personal Habits
Designing Frictionless Loops for Habit Formation
Driving Long-Lasting Behavior Change via Identity Shift
Overcoming Plateaus to Achieve Spiral Personal Growth

title_en: Quit The Power of Knowing When to Walk Away
video_title: S2 61 停損的勝算 Quit The Power of Knowing When to Walk Away
author: Annie Duke
description_cn: Annie Duke結合撲克牌策略與決策科學的著作，探討何時該堅持、何時該停損。提供理性的退出策略框架，幫助讀者避免沉沒成本謬誤，做出更好的人生決策。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 剖析好策略與流於口號的壞策略的本質區別
透過診斷與指導方針聚焦企業核心資源
設計商業護城河以抵禦市場的隨機波動
企業在轉承與接班實務中的風險管理
quotes: 好策略的核心在於聚焦資源，痛擊對手最脆弱、最關鍵的致命處。
企業的基業長青，取決於能否在危機前未雨綢繆地開啟第二增長曲線。
quotes_en: The core of a good strategy is focusing resources to strike the most vulnerable and critical point.
An enterprise's longevity depends on opening a second growth curve before crisis hits.
outline_en: Analyzing the Difference Between Good Strategies and Slogan-Based Bad Strategies
Focusing Core Resources Through System Diagnostics and Guidelines
Designing Business Moats to Protect Against Random Market Fluctuations
Risk Management and Succession Practices in Corporate Transitions

video_title: S2 62 情緒同理 Emotional Empathy
author: Leslie Greenberg
description_cn: 情緒聚焦療法（EFT）一代宗師萊斯利·格林伯格的革命性指南！本書為讀者解開『同理心』的科學運作機制，說明同理心絕非單純的同情，而是深入理解他人情緒、與其痛苦共感，並給予溫柔回應的治癒力量。這本書能幫你建立起深層的情感連結，徹底改善伴侶、親子與職場的人際關係。立即點擊下方連結，開始聆聽如何用情緒同理融化彼此的心牆！
description_en: A revolutionary guide to using empathetic understanding to change each other. When you can understand others' feelings, experience shared pain, and view others as if through a prism, empathy is activated.
outline: 解構自控力與意志力運作的生理與神經科學基礎
對抗短期誘惑、戰勝內心焦慮與壓力的實用工具
發揮內向者的獨特優勢以在喧囂世界中發揮影響力
重建身心能量系統以達成高產出的自我控管
quotes: 意志力不是天生的性格特質，而是像肌肉一樣可以被鍛鍊的生理能力。
真正的專注與平靜，來自於學會在喧囂的世界中尋找屬於自己的安靜力量。
quotes_en: Willpower is not an innate trait, but a physical capacity that can be trained like a muscle.
True focus and peace come from learning to find your quiet strength in a noisy world.
outline_en: Deconstructing the Physiological and Neuroscience Basis of Willpower
Practical Tools to Resist Short-Term Temptations and Conquer Anxiety
Unlocking the Unique Strengths of Introverts to Project Influence
Rebuilding the Mind-Body Energy System for High-Productivity Self-Control

title_cn: 倦怠 為何我們不想工作   波波夫 Exhausted Why We Don’t
video_title: S2 63 倦怠 為何我們不想工作   波波夫 Exhausted Why We Don’t Want to Work
author: 波波夫
description_cn: 資深媒體人與商業觀察家波波夫直擊現代職場痛點的力作！你是否經常感到身心俱疲、對工作失去熱情？本書深度剖析『職場倦怠』背後的社會體制與心理機制，指出倦怠並非因為你不夠努力，而是過度追求效率的必然代價。書中提供具體的自我檢測與調適方案，幫助你在高壓環境中重獲心靈平衡。立即點擊下方連結，聽書學習如何擺脫慢性疲勞、找回工作的初心！
description_en: This is a關於workplace成功和職業發展的practicalguide。The bookcoversleadership、communication skills、teamwork等importanttopics，helpsprofessionals在highly competitive的workplace中stand out。
outline: 識別時間黑洞與拖延的深層根源
掌握高價值任務的優先排序法則
打造無阻力的高效專注執行系統
評估工作效能並優化每日能量分配
quotes: 時間不是被管理的，而是被選擇的。決定不做什麼，往往比決定做什麼更重要。
高效能的秘訣在於專注，而非盲目地將時間表填滿。
quotes_en: Time isn't managed; it is chosen. Deciding what not to do is often more critical than deciding what to do.
The secret of high productivity lies in focus, not blindly filling up your calendar.
outline_en: Identifying Time Sinks and Deep Roots of Procrastination
Mastering Priority Rules for High-Value Tasks
Building a Frictionless, High-Focus Execution System
Evaluating Work Efficiency and Optimizing Daily Energy Allocation

author: 李軒洋
description_cn: 《假面社交》由李軒洋撰寫，揭示現代社交中的虛偽與面具現象。分析人們在社交場合中的心理動機與行為模式，提供更真誠、有效的人際互動方法，幫助讀者建立更真實的關係。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 識別情緒勒索陷阱並設立健康的心理邊界
分析內心對話（Chatter）對情緒與決策的操縱
透過科學方法轉化內心批判為積極動力
培養情緒韌性以應對外界無常的壓力與挑戰
quotes: 善良不是無底線的妥協，你的溫柔必須配上堅強的防禦與健康的邊界。
我們大腦的內心對話，既可以是摧毀自信的雜訊，也可以是引導行動的良師。
quotes_en: Kindness is not bottomless compromise; your warmth must pair with strong defenses and boundaries.
Our inner dialogue can either be a self-destructive noise or a wise mentor guiding action.
outline_en: Identifying Emotional Blackmail Traps and Establishing Healthy Boundaries
Analyzing How Inner Dialogue (Chatter) Manipulates Emotion and Decisions
Using Scientific Methods to Refine Inner Criticism into Positive Motivation
Cultivating Emotional Resilience to Face External Stress and Challenges

title_en: Get Rich Or Lie Trying  Symeon Brown
video_title: S2 65 底層網紅 Get Rich Or Lie Trying  Symeon Brown
author: 希米恩·布朗
description_cn: 《底層網紅：時尚、金錢、性、暴力……社群慾望建構的最強龐氏騙局！》由英國第四頻道新聞記者希米恩·布朗撰寫，透過八篇專題故事揭示歐美網紅市場的黑暗面，涵蓋網紅產業背後的詐騙與剝削。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 解構終身學習大腦的可塑性與發展潛能
建立高效自主的獨學策略與知識內化步驟
利用機率思維在人生重大轉折處進行理性決策

Actively place bets on your life to navigate forward amidst randomness.

Establishing Efficient Strategies for Self-Directed Learning
Using Probabilistic Thinking to Make Choices at Key Transitions
Resetting the Life Blueprint to Launch a Breakthrough Second Half

author: 李檬
description_cn: 天下秀創辦人李檬帶你搶佔新時代流量紅利的商業指南！本書全面拆解『網紅經濟』與『紅人營銷』的底層邏輯，說明品牌如何在新社群時代透過去中心化的紅人矩陣實現銷量暴增，以及創作者如何變現自身影響力。不論你是企業家、行銷經理人還是自媒體創業者，這都是一部不可多得的實戰指南。立即點擊下方連結，聆聽並掌握爆發式增長的網紅經濟學！
description_en: Thiswork為readersreveals了財務自由的path。throughin an accessible manner的方式，作者分享了投資principles、理財strategies和risk management的coreknowledge。
outline: 識別人類大腦天生的心智盲區與思維慣性
建立跨學科多元思維模型以解構複雜問題
提升邏輯推導能力以過濾資訊時代的雜訊
升級思維操作系統以做出明智客觀的決策
quotes: 大腦的認知邊界，就是你人生的邊界。突破舊思維，才能看清局勢。
真正的智慧是學會像科學家一樣思考，隨時質疑自己的既有偏見。
quotes_en: The cognitive boundary of your mind is the boundary of your life. Break old mindsets to see clearly.
True wisdom is learning to think like a scientist, constantly questioning your own biases.
outline_en: Identifying Cognitive Blindspots and Thinking Inertia in the Human Brain
Establishing Multi-Disciplinary Mental Models to Deconstruct Complexity
Enhancing Logical Reasoning to Filter Noise in the Information Age
Upgrading the Mind's OS to Make Wise, Objective Decisions

author: 趙彥春
description_cn: 《譯者即叛徒：翻譯的藝術與困境》探討翻譯過程中不可避免的「創造性背叛」。分析譯者如何在忠實原文與流暢表達之間取得平衡，揭示翻譯作為一門藝術的複雜性與魅力。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 擺脫單一目標導向，建立持續的成長系統

挑戰舒適圈邊緣的難度，大腦的神經連結才會發生實質性改變。
quotes_en: Success is not a one-time event; it is the product of small systems you run daily.
Only by challenging the edge of your comfort zone will neural connections genuinely change.
outline_en: Moving Beyond Single Goals to Build Sustainable Growth Systems
Using Growth Mindset to Transform Mistakes into Brain Fuel
Deconstructing the Daily Training and Energy of Self-Discipline
Optimizing Personal Environment to Reduce Willpower Depletion

title_cn: 子彈溝通   言語武器化 精準吸睛法
video_title: S2 68 子彈溝通   言語武器化 精準吸睛法
author: 何亞妮
description_cn: 暢銷作家何亞妮傳授的精準表達課！在注意力極度碎片化的時代，囉唆就是最大的溝通殺手。本書教你如何將語言『武器化』，運用像子彈般直擊痛點的表達術，在 3 秒內抓住對方眼球、1 分鐘內說服決策者。書中提供大量商務簡報、日常溝通的精準說服模板，特別適合職場人士與銷售精英。立即點擊下方連結，聽書修練你的子彈溝通術！
description_en: This is a提升寫作和表達能力的經典work。The bookprovides了practical的技巧和methods，helpsreaders更clear、更effectively地表達自己的ideas。
outline: 解構傳統對立談判的局限與情緒盲點
建立基於信任與價值創造的談判新框架

理解對方的核心利益與潛在需求，是所有高難度溝通的決勝關鍵。
quotes_en: Negotiation is not a zero-sum game, but a collaborative partnership to create new value.
Understanding the opponent's core interests and underlying needs is key to high-stakes communication.
outline_en: Deconstructing Limits and Emotional Blindspots of Traditional Confrontational Negotiation
Establishing a New Negotiation Framework Based on Trust and Value Creation
Psychological Tactics and Persuasion Skills in High-Stakes Business Communication
How Enterprises Mobilize Collaborative Force Through Effective Culture

title_cn: 總統文膽魅力表達課   江原國

description_en: This is a提升寫作和表達能力的經典work。The bookprovides了practical的技巧和methods，helpsreaders更clear、更effectively地表達自己的ideas。
outline: 探索內在冰山結構以覺察自我情緒起點
掌握深度傾聽與同理心以化解人際衝突
薩提爾模式在親子、伴侶與關係中的應用
與內在脆弱和解以重建健全的自我價值
quotes: 溝通的本質不在於你說了什麼，而在於你是否聽懂了對方冰山底下的真實渴望。
接納不完美是與自己和解的第一步，也是療癒心靈焦慮的起點。
quotes_en: The essence of communication is not what you say, but whether you hear yearnings under the iceberg.
Accepting imperfection is the first step to reconciling with yourself and healing anxiety.
outline_en: Exploring the Iceberg Structure to Awaken the Origins of Self-Emotion
Mastering Deep Listening and Empathy to Resolve Interpersonal Conflicts
Applying Satir's Model in Parent-Child, Partner, and Relationship Scenarios

author: 褚君浩
description_cn: 《重新丈量世界：從測量到科學革命的故事》探討人類如何透過測量來理解和改造世界。從古代的度量衡到現代的精密測量技術，揭示測量如何推動科學進步和文明發展。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 剖析威脅現代人壽命的四大慢性病致命殺手
從運動、營養、睡眠到精神健康的多維長壽方案
探索心靈力量、安慰劑效應與身體治癒的科學證據
制定科學防老與提升晚年生命質量的實操指南
quotes: 健康的長壽不是單純延長壽命，而是要維持高品質的『健康壽命』。
心靈與身體絕非分離，我們的信念與情緒會深刻影響生理免疫系統。
quotes_en: Healthy longevity is not just extending lifespan, but sustaining a high-quality healthspan.
Mind and body are not separate; our beliefs and emotions profoundly shape our immune system.
outline_en: Analyzing the Four Chronic Killers Threatening Modern Lifespan
A Multi-Dimensional Longevity Plan Covering Exercise, Nutrition, and Sleep
Exploring Scientific Evidence of Mind Power and Self-Healing
Formulating Practical Guidelines for Healthy Aging and Lifespan Quality

video_title: S2 73 Growth From Microorganisms to Megacities
author: Vaclav Smil
description_cn: 宏觀科學大師比爾·蓋茲最推崇的作家 Vaclav Smil 的世紀巨著！本書從微生物的繁殖、動植物的發育，到人類城市的擴張以及世界經濟的繁榮，用冷靜客觀的數據與宏大視角，深入探討『成長』的底層規律與自然界不可跨越的極限。這是一部幫你突破認知邊界、用理性的數學眼光審視地球命運的史詩級科普。立即點擊下方連結，聆聽大師對萬物成長機制的深邃思考！
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.
outline_en: Analyzing 52 Most Common Cognitive Errors and Traps in Daily Life
Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

video_title: S2 74 激活增長 Activating Growth
author: 周辰飛, 江竹兵
description_cn: 《激活增長：劇變時代快速增長四步法》由周辰飛和江竹兵合著，系統性地分析企業如何在劇變時代找到增長引擎。提供快速增長的四步法，幫助企業在不確定環境中實現穩定成長。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 市場局勢診斷與企業核心優勢分析
價值主張設計與客戶真實痛點精準定位

商業的本質不是賣產品，而是發現並解決客戶最真實、最隱晦的痛點。
quotes_en: Struggling in price wars of red oceans is mediocre; excellent enterprises focus on blue oceans.
The essence of business is not selling products, but solving customers' deepest pain points.
outline_en: Market Diagnostics and Core Business Strength Analysis
Value Proposition Design and Pinpoint Targeting of Customer Pain Points
Formulating Coherent and Actionable Business Strategies
Creating Uncontested Blue Oceans to Realize Value Innovation

title_en: Empty Planet The Shock of Global Population Decline
video_title: S2 75 無人地球 Empty Planet The Shock of Global Population Decline
author: 達瑞爾·布瑞克, 約翰·伊比森
description_cn: 《無人地球：全面改寫經濟、政治、國際局勢的人口崩潰之戰》探討全球人口下降的趨勢及其深遠影響。從經濟、政治到社會結構，分析人口崩潰將如何徹底改變世界的面貌。

description_cn: 知名財務金融學教授謝劍平的人生財富規劃課！本書將高深的財務管理理論轉化為人人都用得上的理財實務。圍繞儲蓄、資產配置、風險防護、購屋、教育與退休等人生關鍵階段，提出七大財務思考模型，教你如何像企業家一樣管理個人現金流，用財務自由來支撐夢想。適合所有想建立系統化理財邏輯的讀者。立即點擊下方連結，聽書跟著謝教授一起規劃富足人生！
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。

在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.

author: Robert Hagstrom
description_cn: Robert Hagstrom分析巴菲特投資哲學的經典著作，歸納出價值投資的12個核心原則。從企業分析到市場心理，系統性地展現巴菲特的投資智慧。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。

True wisdom is learning to think like a scientist, constantly questioning your own biases.

video_title: S2 90 巴菲特寫給股東的信 The Essays of Warren Buffett   Lessons for Corporate America
author: Warren Buffett
description_cn: 投資界永恆的聖經，股神巴菲特數十年商業與投資智慧的精華集成！這本書由巴菲特親筆撰寫的致股東信整理而成，沒有繁雜的數學公式，只有最質樸、深刻的商業本質思維：如何尋找具有『護城河』的卓越企業、如何面對市場的瘋狂波動，以及如何堅守價值投資的紀律。無論你是股民、創業者還是企業經理人，這都是必讀巨著。立即點擊下方連結，聽書汲取大師的畢生智慧！
description_en: This is a提升寫作和表達能力的經典work。The bookprovides了practical的技巧和methods，helpsreaders更clear、更effectively地表達自己的ideas。

多模型思維能讓你擁有超越常人的視角，在混亂中找到隱藏的秩序。
quotes_en: The world we see is not the real world, but a projection constructed by our mental models.
Multi-model thinking grants you unique perspectives to find hidden order in chaos.
outline_en: Analyzing the Deep Gap Between Subjective Perception and Objective Reality
Learning 32 Multi-Model Strategies and Systems Used by Geniuses
Using Inversion Thinking to Resolve Seemingly Impossible Dilemmas
Establishing Dynamic Feedback to Correct Personal Map of Beliefs

description_cn: 全美推銷大師哈維·麥凱暢銷百萬的經典力作！本書被譽為銷售員與商務人士的實戰聖經。麥凱指出，銷售的本質不是賣產品，而是『賣人際關係與誠信』。書中公開了他著名的『麥凱 66 客戶資料表』，教你如何建立無堅不摧的人脈網絡，並提供無數談判、成交與客戶服務的實用訣竅。適合所有渴望提升業績與溝通影響力的商務人士。立即點擊下方連結，開始聆聽這套銷售必修課！
description_en: This is a關於商業strategies和創業精神的practicalguide。The book分享了successful entrepreneurs的experience和lessons，為aspiringentrepreneursprovides了valuable的advice。

商業的本質不是賣產品，而是發現並解決客戶最真實、最隱晦的痛點。
quotes_en: Struggling in price wars of red oceans is mediocre; excellent enterprises focus on blue oceans.
The essence of business is not selling products, but solving customers' deepest pain points.

description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。

設計商業護城河以抵禦市場的隨機波動

An enterprise's longevity depends on opening a second growth curve before crisis hits.

Designing Business Moats to Protect Against Random Market Fluctuations

author: Lolly Daskal
description_cn: 全球頂尖高管教練 Lolly Daskal 的核心大作！每位領導者都有其獨特的優勢，但也隱藏著阻礙自身卓越的致命盲區。本書揭示了領導力的七種原型（如叛逆者、探索者、真理追求者等）以及對應的陰暗面（如虛張聲勢、偏執等）。教你如何識別自身局限，實行心智與行為模式的成功轉型，釋放最大領袖魅力。適合經理人與團隊領袖。立即點擊下方連結，聽書突破你的領導力天花板！
description_en: This is a關於workplace成功和職業發展的practicalguide。The bookcoversleadership、communication skills、teamwork等importanttopics，helpsprofessionals在highly competitive的workplace中stand out。

大自然隱藏著無數精巧的連結，正如樹木之間透過地底網絡相互照顧。

Nature hides countless exquisite connections, as trees care for each other via underground networks.

title_en: Think Again The Power of Knowing What You Don't Know

author: Adam Grant
description_cn: Adam Grant的暢銷著作，挑戰人們根深蒂固的想法。提出「像科學家一樣思考」的方法，學會質疑自己的信念，以開放心態重新審視世界。
description_en: Adam Grant's bestselling masterpiece that challenges our most deeply-held beliefs. It introduces the concept of 'thinking like a scientist,' teaching us how to question our own assumptions and re-examine the world with an open mind. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
outline: 解構個人思維中的認知盲點與重新思考

像科學家一樣思考：將你的想法視為假說，隨時用新的數據來證偽它。
quotes_en: True intelligence lies not just in how you think, but in your ability to rethink and unlearn your biases.
Think like a scientist: treat your thoughts as hypotheses and constantly use new data to test them.
outline_en: Deconstructing Personal Cognitive Blindspots and Rethinking
How to Guide Others to Rethink with Empathy and Openness
Building a Psychological Safety Culture That Encourages Questioning
Stepping Out of Existing Frameworks to Adapt to a Rapidly Changing World

title_en: Hidden Potential The Science of Achieving Greater Things
video_title: S3 13 隱性潛能  Hidden Potential The Science of Achieving Greater Things
author: Adam Grant
description_cn: Adam Grant最新著作，揭示比天賦更重要的「隱性潛能」。探討支持系統、學習策略和機會創造如何幫助每個人超越自身看似的極限。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 剖析好策略與流於口號的壞策略的本質區別
透過診斷與指導方針聚焦企業核心資源
設計商業護城河以抵禦市場的隨機波動
企業在轉承與接班實務中的風險管理
quotes: 好策略的核心在於聚焦資源，痛擊對手最脆弱、最關鍵的致命處。
企業的基業長青，取決於能否在危機前未雨綢繆地開啟第二增長曲線。
quotes_en: The core of a good strategy is focusing resources to strike the most vulnerable and critical point.
An enterprise's longevity depends on opening a second growth curve before crisis hits.
outline_en: Analyzing the Difference Between Good Strategies and Slogan-Based Bad Strategies
Focusing Core Resources Through System Diagnostics and Guidelines
Designing Business Moats to Protect Against Random Market Fluctuations
Risk Management and Succession Practices in Corporate Transitions

title_en: Outside Insight Navigating a World Drowning in Data
video_title: S3 14 內向外的洞見  Outside Insight Navigating a World Drowning in Data
author: Jørgen Vig Knudstorp
description_cn: Jørgen Vig Knudstorp的商業洞察著作，探討如何利用外部數據與訊號來做出更好的商業決策。在大數據時代，學會從外部世界獲取競爭優勢。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 解構傳統對立談判的局限與情緒盲點
建立基於信任與價值創造的談判新框架
高難度商務溝通中的心理戰術與說服技巧
企業如何透過有效的制度與文化激發協作力
quotes: 談判不是非贏即輸的零和博弈，而是透過創造新價值來達成雙贏的合作。
理解對方的核心利益與潛在需求，是所有高難度溝通的決勝關鍵。
quotes_en: Negotiation is not a zero-sum game, but a collaborative partnership to create new value.
Understanding the opponent's core interests and underlying needs is key to high-stakes communication.
outline_en: Deconstructing Limits and Emotional Blindspots of Traditional Confrontational Negotiation
Establishing a New Negotiation Framework Based on Trust and Value Creation
Psychological Tactics and Persuasion Skills in High-Stakes Business Communication

title_en: Buy Back Your Time Get Unstuck Reclaim Your Freedom and Build Your Empire
video_title: S3 15 把時間買回來 Buy Back Your Time Get Unstuck Reclaim Your Freedom and Build Your Empire
author: Dan Martell
description_cn: 創投大師 Dan Martell 專為創業者與高階經理人量身打造的效率解放指南！許多創業者越成功反而越忙，最終被瑣事淹沒。本書挑戰『忙碌等於成功』的舊觀念，提出創新的『時間買回循環』：教你如何將低價值的日常事務外包或自動化，將省下來的黃金時間重新投入到最具生產力的核心業務中，重獲人身自由與事業成長。立即點擊下方連結，聽書開始拿回你的時間主導權！
description_en: 《把時間買回來》是一部深具inspiration性的work。This bookthroughin an accessible manner的方式，為readers呈現了important的knowledge和insights，值得carefully閱讀。
outline: 識別時間黑洞與拖延的深層根源
掌握高價值任務的優先排序法則
打造無阻力的高效專注執行系統
評估工作效能並優化每日能量分配
quotes: 時間不是被管理的，而是被選擇的。決定不做什麼，往往比決定做什麼更重要。
高效能的秘訣在於專注，而非盲目地將時間表填滿。
quotes_en: Time isn't managed; it is chosen. Deciding what not to do is often more critical than deciding what to do.
The secret of high productivity lies in focus, not blindly filling up your calendar.
outline_en: Identifying Time Sinks and Deep Roots of Procrastination
Mastering Priority Rules for High-Value Tasks
Building a Frictionless, High-Focus Execution System
Evaluating Work Efficiency and Optimizing Daily Energy Allocation

title_cn: 強大內心的自我對話習慣
title_en: Chatter The Voice in Our Head
video_title: S3 16 強大內心的自我對話習慣 Chatter The Voice in Our Head
author: Ethan Kross
description_cn: Ethan Kross的心理學著作，探討內心獨白如何影響我們的情緒、決策和表現。提供科學驗證的方法，將負面的自我對話轉化為強大的內在力量。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 識別情緒勒索陷阱並設立健康的心理邊界
分析內心對話（Chatter）對情緒與決策的操縱
透過科學方法轉化內心批判為積極動力
培養情緒韌性以應對外界無常的壓力與挑戰
quotes: 善良不是無底線的妥協，你的溫柔必須配上堅強的防禦與健康的邊界。
我們大腦的內心對話，既可以是摧毀自信的雜訊，也可以是引導行動的良師。
quotes_en: Kindness is not bottomless compromise; your warmth must pair with strong defenses and boundaries.
Our inner dialogue can either be a self-destructive noise or a wise mentor guiding action.
outline_en: Identifying Emotional Blackmail Traps and Establishing Healthy Boundaries
Analyzing How Inner Dialogue (Chatter) Manipulates Emotion and Decisions
Using Scientific Methods to Refine Inner Criticism into Positive Motivation
Cultivating Emotional Resilience to Face External Stress and Challenges

title_en: The Power of Strangers The Benefits of Connecting in a Suspicious World
video_title: S3 17 陌生人的力量 The Power of Strangers The Benefits of Connecting in a Suspicious World
author: Joe Keohane
description_cn: Joe Keohane探討與陌生人互動的力量的社會學著作。揭示與陌生人交談如何拓展視野、提升幸福感，打破現代社會中人際隔離的困境。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 解構自控力與意志力運作的生理與神經科學基礎
對抗短期誘惑、戰勝內心焦慮與壓力的實用工具

真正的專注與平靜，來自於學會在喧囂的世界中尋找屬於自己的安靜力量。
quotes_en: Willpower is not an innate trait, but a physical capacity that can be trained like a muscle.
True focus and peace come from learning to find your quiet strength in a noisy world.

Practical Tools to Resist Short-Term Temptations and Conquer Anxiety
Unlocking the Unique Strengths of Introverts to Project Influence

title_cn: 為什麼要讀書工作Why do we work
title_en: Important things to think about to be Happy
video_title: S3 18 為什麼要讀書工作Why do we work Important things to think about to be Happy

description_cn: 日本新聞教父池上彰寫給現代職場人與學子的溫暖啟蒙書！我們為什麼要每天辛苦地讀書與工作？工作僅僅是為了賺錢謀生嗎？池上彰以極具親和力的筆觸，從社會協作與個人幸福的宏觀視角，解答了讀書的本質是為了獲得選擇的自由，而工作的本質是與社會建立連結、實現自我價值。這是一本能幫你重燃學習動力、消解工作迷茫的暖心之作。立即點擊下方連結，聽書找回工作的幸福感！
description_en: This is a關於workplace成功和職業發展的practicalguide。The bookcoversleadership、communication skills、teamwork等importanttopics，helpsprofessionals在highly competitive的workplace中stand out。

接納不完美是與自己和解的第一步，也是療癒心靈焦慮的起點。
quotes_en: The essence of communication is not what you say, but whether you hear yearnings under the iceberg.
Accepting imperfection is the first step to reconciling with yourself and healing anxiety.
outline_en: Exploring the Iceberg Structure to Awaken the Origins of Self-Emotion
Mastering Deep Listening and Empathy to Resolve Interpersonal Conflicts
Applying Satir's Model in Parent-Child, Partner, and Relationship Scenarios

title_en: The Vital Question Energy Evolution and the Origins of Complex Life
video_title: S3 19 生命之源  The Vital Question Energy Evolution and the Origins of Complex Life
author: Nick Lane
description_cn: 頂尖生物化學家尼克·連恩的史詩級科學鉅作！為什麼複雜生命在地球上只誕生過一次？連恩指出，生命進化的核心祕密不在於 DNA，而在於『能量流』。他以極具說服力的生物物理學角度，解析線粒體的誕生如何為生命跨越到複雜結構提供能源。這是一部幫你突破認知極限、看懂地球生命奇蹟的必讀著作。立即點擊下方連結，開始聆聽生命起源的終極答案！
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。

學習天才的32個跨界思考策略與系統模型

建立動態反饋機制以修正個人的信念地圖
quotes: 我們看到的世界並非真實世界，而是大腦根據既有模型構建的幻覺。
多模型思維能讓你擁有超越常人的視角，在混亂中找到隱藏的秩序。
quotes_en: The world we see is not the real world, but a projection constructed by our mental models.
Multi-model thinking grants you unique perspectives to find hidden order in chaos.
outline_en: Analyzing the Deep Gap Between Subjective Perception and Objective Reality
Learning 32 Multi-Model Strategies and Systems Used by Geniuses
Using Inversion Thinking to Resolve Seemingly Impossible Dilemmas
Establishing Dynamic Feedback to Correct Personal Map of Beliefs

author: Jessica Eise, Ken Foster
description_cn: 深入解讀全球農業與未來命運的生存指南！面對人口增長與氣候變遷，我們如何餵飽未來的九十億人口？本書系統化解析了現代農業的運作、農產品供應鏈的脆弱性，以及科技與政策在解決糧食危機中的關鍵角色。這不僅是一本糧食科普，更是看懂全球永續發展趨勢的必讀之書。立即點擊下方連結，聽書了解守護地球餐桌的關鍵挑戰！

author: Simon Sinek
description_cn: Simon Sinek提出「無限賽局」概念的領導力著作。區分有限賽局（有明確規則和終點）與無限賽局（持續進行），主張真正的領導者以無限思維經營。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。

真正的智慧是學會像科學家一樣思考，隨時質疑自己的既有偏見。
quotes_en: The cognitive boundary of your mind is the boundary of your life. Break old mindsets to see clearly.
True wisdom is learning to think like a scientist, constantly questioning your own biases.

Establishing Multi-Disciplinary Mental Models to Deconstruct Complexity
Enhancing Logical Reasoning to Filter Noise in the Information Age

description_en: 《決策的藝術》是一部深具inspiration性的work。This bookthroughin an accessible manner的方式，為readers呈現了important的knowledge和insights，值得carefully閱讀。

利用微小行動與『無計劃表』打破僵局
根據大腦能量狀態優化心智帶寬

quotes: 克服拖延的最佳方式，就是立刻跨出第一步，即使是微不足道的行動。
心智管理大於時間管理，順應大腦的能量狀態安排工作才是關鍵。
quotes_en: The best way to beat procrastination is to take the first step immediately, no matter how trivial.
Mind management beats time management; arranging tasks based on brain energy is key.

Breaking Stalemates Using Micro-Actions and Unschedule

description_cn: 皮克斯動畫工作室共同創辦人艾德·卡特姆的管理智慧結晶！皮克斯如何能連續推出《玩具總動員》、《尋夢環遊記》等數十部名利雙收的經典動畫？核心在於激發個人創意的獨特管理文化——『智囊團（Braintrust）』反饋機制、擁抱失敗的誠實態度。這是一本適合所有管理人員、創作者與團隊領袖的創意管理聖經。立即點擊下方連結，聽書了解皮克斯的創意幕後！
description_en: 《創意公司》是一部深具inspiration性的work。This bookthroughin an accessible manner的方式，為readers呈現了important的knowledge和insights，值得carefully閱讀。
outline: 剖析好策略與流於口號的壞策略的本質區別
透過診斷與指導方針聚焦企業核心資源
設計商業護城河以抵禦市場的隨機波動
企業在轉承與接班實務中的風險管理
quotes: 好策略的核心在於聚焦資源，痛擊對手最脆弱、最關鍵的致命處。
企業的基業長青，取決於能否在危機前未雨綢繆地開啟第二增長曲線。
quotes_en: The core of a good strategy is focusing resources to strike the most vulnerable and critical point.
An enterprise's longevity depends on opening a second growth curve before crisis hits.
outline_en: Analyzing the Difference Between Good Strategies and Slogan-Based Bad Strategies
Focusing Core Resources Through System Diagnostics and Guidelines
Designing Business Moats to Protect Against Random Market Fluctuations
Risk Management and Succession Practices in Corporate Transitions

video_title: S3 53 創意自信 Creative Confidence
author: David Kelley, Tom Kelley
description_cn: 全球頂尖設計公司 IDEO 創辦人凱利兄弟的重磅力作！許多人誤以為創意是少數藝術家的天賦，但作者指出，每個人大腦中都藏有無限創意。本書介紹了『設計思考（Design Thinking）』的核心理念，教你如何透過小步快跑的實驗，消除對失敗的恐懼，重建對自身創意的自信。適合想在工作中尋求創新突破的你。立即點擊下方連結，開始聽書釋放你的創意潛能！
description_en: 《創意自信》是一部深具inspiration性的work。This bookthroughin an accessible manner的方式，為readers呈現了important的knowledge和insights，值得carefully閱讀。
outline: 剖析52個日常生活中最常見的認知偏誤與陷阱
擺脫群體盲從，建立獨立客觀的批判性思維
如何透過事實與論據進行嚴謹的理性推理
在不確定性中建立容錯率高的精準決策系統
quotes: 清晰思考是一門需要後天刻意修煉的藝術。別輕易被大眾直覺所綁架。
學會區分事實與觀點，是獨立思考與不盲從的起點。
quotes_en: Clear thinking is an art that requires deliberate practice. Don't be hijacked by popular intuition.
Learning to distinguish facts from opinions is the beginning of independent thinking.
outline_en: Analyzing 52 Most Common Cognitive Errors and Traps in Daily Life
Breaking Free from Herd Mentality to Establish Independent Critical Thinking
Conducting Rigorous Rational Deduction Through Facts and Evidences
Building a High-Fault-Tolerance Precision Decision System Under Uncertainty

video_title: S3 54 斯多葛主義小書 The Little Book of Stoicism
author: Jonas Salzgeber
description_cn: Jonas Salzgeber的斯多葛哲學入門指南，將古希臘羅馬的智慧應用於現代生活。提供實用的練習方法，幫助讀者培養韌性、掌控情緒、活出有意義的人生。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 識別人類大腦天生的心智盲區與思維慣性
建立跨學科多元思維模型以解構複雜問題
提升邏輯推導能力以過濾資訊時代的雜訊
升級思維操作系統以做出明智客觀的決策
quotes: 大腦的認知邊界，就是你人生的邊界。突破舊思維，才能看清局勢。
真正的智慧是學會像科學家一樣思考，隨時質疑自己的既有偏見。
quotes_en: The cognitive boundary of your mind is the boundary of your life. Break old mindsets to see clearly.
True wisdom is learning to think like a scientist, constantly questioning your own biases.
outline_en: Identifying Cognitive Blindspots and Thinking Inertia in the Human Brain
Establishing Multi-Disciplinary Mental Models to Deconstruct Complexity
Enhancing Logical Reasoning to Filter Noise in the Information Age
Upgrading the Mind's OS to Make Wise, Objective Decisions

title_cn: 改善-改變命運的一小步
title_en: One Small Step Can Change Your Life  The Kaizen Way
video_title: S3 55 改善-改變命運的一小步 One Small Step Can Change Your Life  The Kaizen Way
author: Robert Maurer
description_cn: Robert Maurer的漸進式改變方法論，介紹源自日本的「改善」（Kaizen）哲學。強調透過微小的、不引起恐懼的步驟，就能繞過大腦的抗拒機制，實現持久的改變。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 剖析主觀認知與客觀事實之間的巨大鴻溝
學習天才的32個跨界思考策略與系統模型
如何利用逆向思維解開看似無解的兩難困境
建立動態反饋機制以修正個人的信念地圖
quotes: 我們看到的世界並非真實世界，而是大腦根據既有模型構建的幻覺。
多模型思維能讓你擁有超越常人的視角，在混亂中找到隱藏的秩序。
quotes_en: The world we see is not the real world, but a projection constructed by our mental models.
Multi-model thinking grants you unique perspectives to find hidden order in chaos.
outline_en: Analyzing the Deep Gap Between Subjective Perception and Objective Reality
Learning 32 Multi-Model Strategies and Systems Used by Geniuses
Using Inversion Thinking to Resolve Seemingly Impossible Dilemmas
Establishing Dynamic Feedback to Correct Personal Map of Beliefs

video_title: S3 56 折棉被吧 ! Make Your Bed  Little Things That Can Change Your Life and Maybe the World
author: Ryan Holiday
description_cn: William H. McRaven海軍上將的勵志著作，從海豹部隊訓練中提煉10個改變人生的原則。第一個就是「每天從折棉被開始」，以小紀律建立大成就。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 解構終身學習大腦的可塑性與發展潛能
建立高效自主的獨學策略與知識內化步驟
利用機率思維在人生重大轉折處進行理性決策
重置生活指南以開啟自我突破的下半場人生
quotes: 最好的投資是投資自己的大腦。持續的自我疊代是抵禦不確定性的唯一武器。
主動為自己的人生下注，在隨機性中尋找前行的方向。
quotes_en: The best investment is in your mind. Continuous iteration is your shield against uncertainty.
Actively place bets on your life to navigate forward amidst randomness.

Establishing Efficient Strategies for Self-Directed Learning
Using Probabilistic Thinking to Make Choices at Key Transitions
Resetting the Life Blueprint to Launch a Breakthrough Second Half

author: Lachlan Brown
description_cn: Lachlan Brown的正向心理學著作，重新定義「舒適區」的價值。提出正向改變不一定要來自痛苦，學會在舒適中找到成長的關鍵。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 探索內在冰山結構以覺察自我情緒起點
掌握深度傾聽與同理心以化解人際衝突
薩提爾模式在親子、伴侶與關係中的應用
與內在脆弱和解以重建健全的自我價值
quotes: 溝通的本質不在於你說了什麼，而在於你是否聽懂了對方冰山底下的真實渴望。
接納不完美是與自己和解的第一步，也是療癒心靈焦慮的起點。
quotes_en: The essence of communication is not what you say, but whether you hear yearnings under the iceberg.
Accepting imperfection is the first step to reconciling with yourself and healing anxiety.
outline_en: Exploring the Iceberg Structure to Awaken the Origins of Self-Emotion
Mastering Deep Listening and Empathy to Resolve Interpersonal Conflicts
Applying Satir's Model in Parent-Child, Partner, and Relationship Scenarios
Reconciling with Inner Vulnerability to Rebuild Healthy Self-Worth

title_en: The Comfort Zone  Your Path to Lasting Success
video_title: S3 58 別再脫離舒適區 The Comfort Zone  Your Path to Lasting Success
author: Farrah Storr
description_cn: Farrah Storr挑戰「跳出舒適區」迷思的著作，提出在舒適區邊緣擴張才是持久成長的方法。分享如何以自己的節奏漸進式地擴展能力邊界。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 識別情緒勒索陷阱並設立健康的心理邊界
分析內心對話（Chatter）對情緒與決策的操縱
透過科學方法轉化內心批判為積極動力
培養情緒韌性以應對外界無常的壓力與挑戰
quotes: 善良不是無底線的妥協，你的溫柔必須配上堅強的防禦與健康的邊界。
我們大腦的內心對話，既可以是摧毀自信的雜訊，也可以是引導行動的良師。
quotes_en: Kindness is not bottomless compromise; your warmth must pair with strong defenses and boundaries.
Our inner dialogue can either be a self-destructive noise or a wise mentor guiding action.
outline_en: Identifying Emotional Blackmail Traps and Establishing Healthy Boundaries
Analyzing How Inner Dialogue (Chatter) Manipulates Emotion and Decisions
Using Scientific Methods to Refine Inner Criticism into Positive Motivation

title_cn: 3 Minute Positivity Journal - Boost Your Mood^J Change Your Life
video_title: S3 59 3 Minute Positivity Journal - Boost Your Mood^J Change Your Life
author: Kristen Butler
description_cn: Kristen Butler的正向心理學日記工具書，透過每日三分鐘的正向書寫練習，幫助讀者培養感恩、樂觀和自我覺察的習慣，持續提升幸福感。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 解構自控力與意志力運作的生理與神經科學基礎
對抗短期誘惑、戰勝內心焦慮與壓力的實用工具
發揮內向者的獨特優勢以在喧囂世界中發揮影響力
重建身心能量系統以達成高產出的自我控管
quotes: 意志力不是天生的性格特質，而是像肌肉一樣可以被鍛鍊的生理能力。
真正的專注與平靜，來自於學會在喧囂的世界中尋找屬於自己的安靜力量。
quotes_en: Willpower is not an innate trait, but a physical capacity that can be trained like a muscle.
True focus and peace come from learning to find your quiet strength in a noisy world.
outline_en: Deconstructing the Physiological and Neuroscience Basis of Willpower
Practical Tools to Resist Short-Term Temptations and Conquer Anxiety
Unlocking the Unique Strengths of Introverts to Project Influence
Rebuilding the Mind-Body Energy System for High-Productivity Self-Control

video_title: S3 60 擁抱同理心 Embrace Your Empathy  Making Sensitivity Your Strength
author: Daniel Goleman
description_cn: Daniel Goleman的情商著作，探討同理心在領導力、人際關係和社會連結中的核心作用。提供培養同理心的實用方法，幫助建立更深層的人際連結。
description_en: This is a關於個人發展和自我提升的work。This bookprovidespractical的strategies和profound的insights，helpsreaders在生活和工作中實現更高的成就。
outline: 市場局勢診斷與企業核心優勢分析
價值主張設計與客戶真實痛點精準定位
制定具備連貫性與高執行力的商業戰略
開創無競爭藍海市場以實現價值創新
quotes: 在紅海的價格戰中掙扎是平庸的選擇，卓越的企業專注於開創藍海與價值創新。
商業的本質不是賣產品，而是發現並解決客戶最真實、最隱晦的痛點。
quotes_en: Struggling in price wars of red oceans is mediocre; excellent enterprises focus on blue oceans.
The essence of business is not selling products, but solving customers' deepest pain points.
outline_en: Market Diagnostics and Core Business Strength Analysis
Value Proposition Design and Pinpoint Targeting of Customer Pain Points
Formulating Coherent and Actionable Business Strategies
Creating Uncontested Blue Oceans to Realize Value Innovation

author: 嶋田毅

description_en: This is a關於商業strategies和創業精神的practicalguide。The book分享了successful entrepreneurs的experience和lessons，為aspiringentrepreneursprovides了valuable的advice。
outline: 剖析主觀認知與客觀事實之間的巨大鴻溝

Multi-model thinking grants you unique perspectives to find hidden order in chaos.

title_en: The Art of Reading Minds  Understand Others
video_title: S3 62 讀新的藝術 The Art of Reading Minds  Understand Others
author: Mortimer J. Adler
description_cn: 讀心術大師 Henrik Fexeus 教你的日常身體語言洞察術！在人際溝通與商務談判中，如何知道對方真正的想法與底牌？本書教你如何透過微表情、眼神、站姿以及語氣的變化，解讀他人內心隱秘的真實感受。同時，作者也分享如何運用特定的肢體語言建立默契、贏得信任，在無形中發揮你的影響力。特別適合銷售與主管。立即點擊下方連結，聽書學習這套人際讀心術！
description_en: 《讀新的藝術》是一部深具inspiration性的work。This bookthroughin an accessible manner的方式，為readers呈現了important的knowledge和insights，值得carefully閱讀。

理解對方的核心利益與潛在需求，是所有高難度溝通的決勝關鍵。
quotes_en: Negotiation is not a zero-sum game, but a collaborative partnership to create new value.
Understanding the opponent's core interests and underlying needs is key to high-stakes communication.
outline_en: Deconstructing Limits and Emotional Blindspots of Traditional Confrontational Negotiation
Establishing a New Negotiation Framework Based on Trust and Value Creation
Psychological Tactics and Persuasion Skills in High-Stakes Business Communication
How Enterprises Mobilize Collaborative Force Through Effective Culture

description_en: A practical mind-decluttering guide by S.J. Scott, offering effective strategies to eliminate anxiety, worry, and negative thoughts. Through mindfulness and cognitive restructuring, it helps readers establish a clearer, more focused state of mind. Click the link below to listen to this inspiring book summary!
